fields.text: Which of the following has a laterally compressed body and no wings?
fields.explanation: Fleas have laterally compressed bodies (flattened from side to side) and are wingless. This body shape helps them move through host fur/hair.

fields.text: Sexes are separate in which of the following?
fields.explanation: Explanation: Amphipods are dioecious (separate sexes). Schistosomes also have separate sexes, but amphipods is the better answer here.

fields.text: Nematodes are best fixed in:
fields.explanation: Hot glycerine alcohol (70% alcohol + glycerine, heated) is the preferred fixative for nematodes as it prevents hardening and maintains morphology.

fields.text: Gallamine Mustibe Test is carried out in:
fields.explanation: This appears to be a milk quality test, though the exact spelling may be unclear in the source.

fields.text: Commercially available concentrated formaldehyde is:
fields.explanation: Commercial formalin is typically 37-40% formaldehyde solution.

fields.text: Man, definitive host, sporocyst, redia, cercaria, metacercaria are seen in the life cycle of:
fields.explanation: This life cycle with sporocyst, redia, cercaria, and metacercaria stages is characteristic of trematodes (flukes).

fields.text: The eggs of which parasite has a Napoleon hat shape?
fields.explanation: Trichuris trichiura (whipworm) eggs have a characteristic barrel or "Napoleon hat" shape with polar plugs.

fields.text: In Male Anopheles, palps are:

options.A: As long as proboscis with 4 segments

fields.explanation: Male Anopheles mosquitoes have palps that are as long as the proboscis with 4 segments.

fields.text: Which microorganism is resistant to most antimicrobials except for nitrofurantoin for urinary tract infections?
fields.explanation: Pseudomonas aeruginosa is notoriously resistant to multiple antibiotics but can be sensitive to nitrofurantoin in UTIs.

fields.text: Which has the pear shaped operculate eggs:
fields.explanation: Clonorchis sinensis eggs are small, operculated, and pear-shaped.

fields.text: For fixation of tissues, the fixative should be:
fields.explanation: 10% neutral buffered formalin is the standard fixative for routine histopathology.

fields.text: The part of a microscope that serves to regulate light entering the condenser is:
fields.explanation: The iris diaphragm controls the amount of light entering the condenser.

fields.text: Which of the following fixative contains picric acid, formaldehyde and glacial acetic acid?
fields.explanation: Bouin's solution contains picric acid, formaldehyde, and glacial acetic acid.

fields.text: For fixation, the recommended quantity of the fixative should be:

options.A: 20 times the volume of the tissue
options.B: Half the volume of the tissue
options.C: 100 times the volume of the tissue
options.D: Equal to the volume of tissue

fields.explanation: The fixative volume should be 10-20 times the tissue volume for proper fixation.

fields.text: The unit for expressing sedimentation rate (ESR) is:
fields.explanation: ESR is measured in mm/hour (Westergren method).

fields.text: The old name of aspartate aminotransferase (AST) is:
fields.explanation: AST was formerly called SGOT (Serum Glutamic Oxaloacetic Transaminase).

fields.text: Which is not a liver function test?
fields.explanation: Serum creatinine is a kidney function test, not a liver function test.

fields.text: An example of a special staining is:
fields.explanation: All are special stains, but PAS is commonly used for glycogen and mucins.

fields.text: The sterilization conditions in a steam jacketed autoclave is:
fields.explanation: Standard autoclave conditions are 121°C at 15 psi for 15 minutes.

fields.text: The special microscope used for examining spirochaetes in blood sample is:
fields.explanation: Dark field microscopy is used to visualize spirochaetes like Treponema pallidum.

fields.text: The incorporation percentage of agar in common bacteriological media is:
fields.explanation: Most solid bacteriological media contain 1.5-2% agar.

fields.text: The most common enzyme used in a polymerase chain reaction is:
fields.explanation: Taq polymerase from Thermus aquaticus is heat-stable and most commonly used in PCR.

fields.text: The agar recommended for antibiotic sensitivity testing is:
fields.explanation: Mueller Hinton agar is the standard medium for antimicrobial susceptibility testing.

fields.text: Urinary crystals having coffin lid appearance is:
fields.explanation: Triple phosphate crystals (magnesium ammonium phosphate) have a characteristic coffin lid appearance.

fields.text: Urobilinogen is completely absent in:
fields.explanation: In complete biliary obstruction, no bilirubin reaches the intestine, so no urobilinogen is formed.

fields.text: Black coloured urine is a feature of:
fields.explanation: Alkaptonuria causes darkening of urine on standing due to homogentisic acid.

fields.text: Length of Wintrobe's haematocrit tube:
fields.explanation: Wintrobe hematocrit tubes are 100mm long with 0-10 graduations.

fields.text: Coulter counter principle is based on:
fields.explanation: Coulter counters work on the impedance principle - cells passing through an aperture change electrical resistance.

fields.text: Weight of NaOH to be taken for preparing 0.2 N NaOH in 100 ml of quantity is: [Mol. wt. NaOH 40]
fields.explanation: Normality = (Weight × 1000)/(Mol. wt. × Volume in ml). 0.2 = (W × 1000)/(40 × 100), W = 0.8g

fields.text: Bence-Jones protein is seen in:
fields.explanation: Bence-Jones proteins are free immunoglobulin light chains seen in multiple myeloma.

fields.text: Antibiotic affecting DNA gyrase action:
fields.explanation: Quinolones inhibit bacterial DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV.

fields.text: Which among the following is a best indicator of myocardial infarction?
fields.explanation: Cardiac troponins are the most specific and sensitive markers for myocardial infarction.

fields.text: Microalbuminuria is defined as:
fields.explanation: Microalbuminuria is defined as 30-300 mg albumin excretion per day.

fields.text: HbA1C level 4.5%-6% indicates:
fields.explanation: Normal HbA1C is less than 5.7%, prediabetes is 5.7-6.4%, diabetes is ≥6.5%.

fields.text: Which among the following is an in vitro gene amplification procedure?
fields.explanation: PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) is the standard in vitro gene amplification technique.

fields.text: KOH preparation is used to demonstrate:
fields.explanation: KOH (Potassium Hydroxide) mount is used to visualize fungal elements by dissolving cellular debris.

fields.text: Who led the Anti-British struggle in Kittur?
fields.explanation: Rani Chennamma of Kittur led one of the first armed resistances against British rule in 1824.

fields.text: The Mughal Architecture is a blending of:
fields.explanation: Mughal architecture combines Persian, Turkish, and Indian architectural elements.

fields.text: The Article 21 of Indian constitution guarantees:
fields.explanation: Article 21 guarantees the fundamental right to life and personal liberty.

fields.text: Who was the author of Kalpasutra?
fields.explanation: Kalpasutra is a Jain text traditionally attributed to Acharya Bhadrabahu.

fields.text: First woman judge in Supreme Court of India:
fields.explanation: Justice M. Fathima Beevi was the first woman judge of the Supreme Court of India (1989).

fields.text: Who described Tajmahal as "A tear drop on the cheek of eternity"?
fields.explanation: This famous poetic description of the Taj Mahal is attributed to Rabindranath Tagore.

fields.text: The first general election after the formation of Kerala state was held in:
fields.explanation: Kerala's first assembly election was held in February 1957 after the state's formation in 1956.

fields.text: Who is the Chief Minister of Bihar?
fields.explanation: As of 2016, Nitish Kumar was the Chief Minister of Bihar.

fields.text: Where is the headquarters of Western Command of Indian Army?
fields.explanation: The Western Command of the Indian Army is headquartered at Chandigarh.

fields.text: The infective stage of Entamoeba histolytica is:
fields.explanation: The mature quadrinucleate cyst is the infective stage of E. histolytica, transmitted via fecal-oral route.

fields.text: Which mosquito is the vector for dengue fever?
fields.explanation: Aedes aegypti is the primary vector for dengue virus transmission.

fields.text: The laboratory diagnosis of malaria is best done by:
fields.explanation: Microscopic examination of Giemsa-stained thick and thin blood smears remains the gold standard for malaria diagnosis.

fields.text: Which parasite causes sleeping sickness?
fields.explanation: Trypanosoma brucei causes African sleeping sickness (African trypanosomiasis).

fields.text: The definitive host for Taenia solium is:
fields.explanation: Humans are the definitive host for T. solium (pork tapeworm), while pigs serve as intermediate hosts.

fields.text: Charcot-Leyden crystals in stool are associated with:
fields.explanation: Charcot-Leyden crystals are formed from eosinophil proteins and indicate parasitic infections.

fields.text: The causative agent of kala-azar is:
fields.explanation: Leishmania donovani causes visceral leishmaniasis (kala-azar).

fields.text: Which stain is commonly used for demonstrating parasites in stool?
fields.explanation: Iodine wet mount helps visualize cysts and trophozoites of intestinal parasites.

fields.text: The intermediate host for Schistosoma mansoni is:
fields.explanation: Freshwater snails serve as intermediate hosts for Schistosoma species.

fields.text: Pinworm infection is diagnosed by:
fields.explanation: The cellotape (scotch tape) test is used to collect Enterobius vermicularis eggs from the perianal area.

fields.text: The most common cause of urinary tract infection is:
fields.explanation: E. coli accounts for 80-85% of uncomplicated urinary tract infections.

fields.text: MacConkey agar is selective for:
fields.explanation: MacConkey agar contains bile salts and crystal violet that inhibit gram-positive bacteria.

fields.text: The causative agent of whooping cough is:
fields.explanation: Bordetella pertussis causes pertussis (whooping cough).

fields.text: Catalase test is positive in:
fields.explanation: Staphylococci are catalase positive, while streptococci are catalase negative.

fields.text: The temperature for incubation of most pathogenic bacteria is:
fields.explanation: 37°C is the optimal temperature for most human pathogenic bacteria as it mimics body temperature.

fields.text: Mannitol salt agar is used for isolation of:
fields.explanation: Mannitol salt agar is selective for staphylococci due to high salt concentration.

fields.text: The most appropriate specimen for whooping cough diagnosis is:
fields.explanation: Nasopharyngeal swab is the specimen of choice for B. pertussis isolation.

fields.text: Coagulase test is used to differentiate:
fields.explanation: Coagulase test differentiates S. aureus (positive) from coagulase-negative staphylococci.

fields.text: The causative agent of gas gangrene is:
fields.explanation: Clostridium perfringens is the most common cause of gas gangrene.

fields.text: Oxidase test is positive in:
fields.explanation: Pseudomonas aeruginosa is oxidase positive, while most Enterobacteriaceae are oxidase negative.

fields.text: The most common cause of bacterial meningitis in adults is:
fields.explanation: S. pneumoniae is the leading cause of bacterial meningitis in adults.

fields.text: Blood agar is an example of:
fields.explanation: Blood agar is an enriched medium that supports the growth of fastidious organisms.

fields.text: The causative agent of typhoid fever is:
fields.explanation: Salmonella typhi causes typhoid fever.

fields.text: Beta-hemolysis on blood agar appears as:
fields.explanation: Beta-hemolysis causes complete lysis of red blood cells, creating clear zones around colonies.

fields.text: The specimen of choice for tuberculosis diagnosis is:
fields.explanation: Early morning sputum is the preferred specimen for pulmonary tuberculosis diagnosis.

fields.text: Gram staining was developed by:
fields.explanation: The Gram stain was developed by Danish bacteriologist Hans Christian Gram in 1884.

fields.text: The most common cause of food poisoning is:
fields.explanation: Salmonella species are the most common bacterial cause of foodborne illness.

fields.text: Optochin sensitivity test is used for identification of:
fields.explanation: S. pneumoniae is sensitive to optochin, which helps differentiate it from other alpha-hemolytic streptococci.

fields.text: The primary stain in Gram staining is:
fields.explanation: Crystal violet is the primary stain that colors all bacteria purple initially.

fields.text: Which organism causes woolsorter's disease?
fields.explanation: Pulmonary anthrax caused by B. anthracis was historically called woolsorter's disease.

fields.text: Chocolate agar is prepared by heating blood agar to:
fields.explanation: Chocolate agar is prepared by heating blood agar to 80°C to lyse red blood cells.

fields.text: The causative agent of plague is:
fields.explanation: Yersinia pestis causes bubonic and pneumonic plague.

fields.text: Bacitracin sensitivity test is used for identification of:
fields.explanation: S. pyogenes (Group A Streptococcus) is sensitive to bacitracin.

fields.text: The most common cause of neonatal meningitis is:
fields.explanation: Group B Streptococcus (S. agalactiae) is a leading cause of neonatal meningitis.

fields.text: Spore stain is also known as:
fields.explanation: The spore stain uses malachite green as the primary stain to visualize bacterial spores.

fields.text: The causative agent of diphtheria is:
fields.explanation: Corynebacterium diphtheriae causes diphtheria.

fields.text: Which medium is used for isolation of Vibrio cholerae?
fields.explanation: TCBS (Thiosulfate Citrate Bile Salt Sucrose) agar is selective for Vibrio species.

fields.text: Lancefield grouping is used for classification of:
fields.explanation: Lancefield grouping classifies beta-hemolytic streptococci based on C carbohydrate antigen.

fields.text: The causative agent of tularemia is:
fields.explanation: Francisella tularensis causes tularemia (rabbit fever).

fields.text: Urease test is positive in:
fields.explanation: Proteus species are strongly urease positive, helping in their identification.

fields.text: Normal hemoglobin level in adult males is:
fields.explanation: Normal hemoglobin in adult males ranges from 14-18 g/dL.

fields.text: The normal range for total leukocyte count is:
fields.explanation: Normal total WBC count ranges from 4,000-11,000 cells per microliter.

fields.text: Reticulocyte count indicates:
fields.explanation: Reticulocyte count reflects bone marrow erythropoietic activity.

fields.text: Which enzyme is elevated in acute pancreatitis?
fields.explanation: Serum amylase and lipase are elevated in acute pancreatitis.

fields.text: Normal fasting blood glucose level is:
fields.explanation: Normal fasting blood glucose ranges from 70-110 mg/dL (3.9-6.1 mmol/L).

fields.text: The anticoagulant used for ESR estimation is:
fields.explanation: Sodium citrate is the anticoagulant of choice for ESR estimation.

fields.text: Jaundice becomes clinically apparent when serum bilirubin exceeds:
fields.explanation: Clinical jaundice is visible when serum bilirubin exceeds 2-2.5 mg/dL.

fields.text: Osmotic fragility test is used to diagnose:
fields.explanation: Osmotic fragility test detects hereditary spherocytosis due to increased membrane fragility.

fields.text: Normal platelet count ranges from:
fields.explanation: Normal platelet count ranges from 150,000-450,000 per microliter.

fields.text: The most abundant plasma protein is:
fields.explanation: Albumin comprises about 60% of total plasma proteins.

fields.text: Coombs test is used to detect:
fields.explanation: Direct Coombs test detects antibodies or complement bound to RBC surface.

fields.text: Normal serum creatinine level is:
fields.explanation: Normal serum creatinine ranges from 0.6-1.2 mg/dL in adults.

fields.text: Reed-Sternberg cells are characteristic of:
fields.explanation: Reed-Sternberg cells are pathognomonic for Hodgkin's lymphoma.

fields.text: Which test is used to monitor warfarin therapy?
fields.explanation: Prothrombin time (PT) and International Normalized Ratio (INR) monitor warfarin therapy.

fields.text: Hypochromic microcytic anemia is seen in:
fields.explanation: Iron deficiency anemia presents as hypochromic microcytic anemia.

fields.text: Normal BUN (Blood Urea Nitrogen) level is:
fields.explanation: Normal BUN ranges from 10-20 mg/dL (3.6-7.1 mmol/L urea).

fields.text: Auer rods are seen in:
fields.explanation: Auer rods are pathognomonic for acute myeloid leukemia.

fields.text: The primary function of albumin is:
fields.explanation: Albumin maintains plasma oncotic pressure and transports various substances.

fields.text: Heinz bodies are seen in:
fields.explanation: Heinz bodies (denatured hemoglobin) are seen in G6PD deficiency after oxidative stress.

fields.text: Normal serum sodium level is:
fields.explanation: Normal serum sodium ranges from 135-145 mEq/L (mmol/L).

fields.text: Philadelphia chromosome is associated with:
fields.explanation: Philadelphia chromosome t(9;22) is characteristic of chronic myeloid leukemia.

fields.text: Schilling test is used to diagnose:
fields.explanation: Schilling test determines the cause of vitamin B12 deficiency.

fields.text: Normal serum potassium level is:
fields.explanation: Normal serum potassium ranges from 3.5-5.0 mEq/L (mmol/L).

fields.text: Target cells are characteristic of:
fields.explanation: Target cells (codocytes) are commonly seen in thalassemia and liver disease.

fields.text: Normal serum chloride level is:
fields.explanation: Normal serum chloride ranges from 96-106 mEq/L (mmol/L).

fields.text: Name an indigenous breed of Duck?
fields.explanation: Chara is an indigenous breed of duck, commonly found in India, known for its adaptability to local conditions. Vigova is a commercial hybrid, and Rhode Island Red is a chicken breed, not a duck.

fields.text: Who monitors the LMICS of the state?
fields.explanation: The Livestock Management Information and Communication System (LMICS) of the state is monitored by the PTO at Kudappanakanna, as per the Animal Husbandry Department structure.

fields.text: Which Regional Director laboratory has jurisdiction over Idukki, Kottayam, Pathanamthitta, and Alappuzha?
fields.explanation: The Additional Director of Laboratory (ADDL) at Thiruvalla has jurisdiction over the districts of Idukki, Kottayam, Pathanamthitta, and Alappuzha for laboratory services.

fields.text: Feed compounding factory is established in?
fields.explanation: The feed compounding factory is established at the Poultry Farm in Malampuzha, which is equipped for producing compounded feed for livestock.

fields.text: Number of Administrative Assistants in the Directorate?
fields.explanation: The Directorate of the Animal Husbandry Department has three Administrative Assistants as part of its administrative structure.

fields.text: Details of staff should be reported?
fields.explanation: Staff details in the Animal Husbandry Department are required to be reported on a yearly basis for administrative and record-keeping purposes.

fields.text: Every veterinary institution should have an office seal containing Government emblem, name, and?
fields.explanation: The office seal of a veterinary institution must include the Government emblem, the name of the institution, and the place where it is located.

fields.text: Which one of the following institutions is handed over to Block Panchayat?
fields.explanation: Veterinary Subcentres are typically handed over to Block Panchayats for decentralized management under local governance.

fields.text: The rank of Standardization of officer AH & VB?
fields.explanation: The standardization officer in the Animal Husbandry and Veterinary Board (AH & VB) holds the rank of Deputy Director.

fields.text: Which one of the following is the sample of choice for diagnosis of Anaplasmosis?
fields.explanation: Blood is the sample of choice for diagnosing Anaplasmosis, as it allows detection of the causative agent, Anaplasma, via microscopy or PCR.

fields.text: In which one of the following institutions, Assistant Director (Rabies Eradication) is posted?
fields.explanation: The Assistant Director (Rabies Eradication) is posted at the Directorate, overseeing state-level rabies control programs.

fields.text: The cash book should be maintained in the manner prescribed as per which article of the Kerala Treasury Code?
fields.explanation: The maintenance of the cash book is prescribed under Article 90 of the Kerala Treasury Code, which outlines financial record-keeping procedures.

fields.text: PPR vaccine given to goats confirms immunity for?
fields.explanation: The PPR (Peste des Petits Ruminants) vaccine provides immunity to goats for up to 3 years, offering long-term protection against the disease.

fields.text: Name the referral Veterinary health centre at District level?
fields.explanation: The District Veterinary Centre serves as the referral veterinary health centre at the district level, providing advanced care and diagnostics.

fields.text: Puliorum disease in poultry is caused by?
fields.explanation: Puliorum disease in poultry is caused by Salmonella pullorum, a bacterial pathogen affecting young chicks, leading to high mortality.

fields.text: Which of the following is an indigenous breed of buffalo?
fields.explanation: Murrah is an indigenous Indian buffalo breed, known for high milk yield. Jersey, Holstein, and Brown Swiss are exotic cattle breeds, not buffaloes.

fields.text: What is the causative agent of Foot and Mouth Disease (FMD) in cattle?
fields.explanation: Foot and Mouth Disease is caused by a virus from the Picornaviridae family, affecting cloven-hoofed animals like cattle.

fields.text: Which vaccine is used to control Brucellosis in cattle?
fields.explanation: The RB51 vaccine is used to control Brucellosis in cattle, caused by Brucella abortus, to prevent abortions and infertility.

fields.text: Which poultry disease is also known as Ranikhet disease?
fields.explanation: Newcastle Disease, caused by a paramyxovirus, is also called Ranikhet disease in India, affecting poultry with respiratory and nervous symptoms.

fields.text: What is the primary source of energy in poultry feed?
fields.explanation: Carbohydrates, mainly from grains like maize, provide the primary energy source in poultry feed for growth and egg production.

fields.text: Which officer is responsible for overseeing veterinary hospitals at the district level?
fields.explanation: The Chief Veterinary Officer oversees veterinary hospitals at the district level, managing clinical and administrative functions.

fields.text: What is the gestation period of a cow?
fields.explanation: The gestation period of a cow is approximately 280 days, varying slightly by breed and individual.

fields.text: Which of the following is a zoonotic disease?
fields.explanation: Anthrax, caused by Bacillus anthracis, is a zoonotic disease transmissible to humans through contact with infected animals or their products.

fields.text: What is the main purpose of dehorning in cattle?
fields.explanation: Dehorning prevents injuries to other animals and handlers, improving safety in cattle management.

fields.text: Which mineral deficiency causes grass tetany in cattle?
fields.explanation: Grass tetany, or hypomagnesemia, is caused by magnesium deficiency, often in cattle grazing on lush pastures.

fields.text: Which institution maintains the State Poultry Farm in Kerala?
fields.explanation: The Animal Husbandry Department of Kerala maintains State Poultry Farms for breeding and supply of poultry.

fields.text: What is the incubation period of Infectious Bursal Disease (IBD) in poultry?
fields.explanation: Infectious Bursal Disease, or Gumboro disease, has an incubation period of 2–3 days, affecting young chickens' immune systems.

fields.text: Which record is mandatory for every veterinary hospital as per Kerala Veterinary Rules?
fields.explanation: The Treatment Register is mandatory to record details of animals treated, as per Kerala Veterinary Rules.

fields.text: What is the primary vector for Trypanosomiasis in cattle?
fields.explanation: Trypanosomiasis, or sleeping sickness, is transmitted by the tsetse fly, affecting cattle in certain regions.

fields.text: Which of the following is a dual-purpose chicken breed?
fields.explanation: Rhode Island Red is a dual-purpose breed, valued for both egg production and meat, unlike Leghorn (eggs) or Cornish (meat).

fields.text: Which of the following is a viral disease affecting poultry?
fields.explanation: Avian Influenza, caused by influenza A viruses, is a viral disease affecting poultry, leading to respiratory and systemic symptoms. Fowl Cholera and Salmonellosis are bacterial, while Coccidiosis is protozoal.

fields.text: What is the primary purpose of the Kerala Livestock Development Board (KLDB)?
fields.explanation: The KLDB focuses on livestock breeding and genetic improvement to enhance productivity, particularly in cattle and poultry.

fields.text: Which of the following is an indigenous goat breed of India?
fields.explanation: Jamnapari is an indigenous Indian goat breed known for milk and meat production. Boer, Saanen, and Alpine are exotic breeds.

fields.text: What is the causative agent of Mastitis in dairy cattle?
fields.explanation: Mastitis in dairy cattle is primarily caused by bacteria, such as Staphylococcus aureus or Streptococcus species, leading to udder inflammation.

fields.text: Which vitamin deficiency causes rickets in young animals?
fields.explanation: Vitamin D deficiency causes rickets in young animals, leading to weak bones and skeletal deformities due to impaired calcium absorption.

fields.text: Which institution is responsible for issuing veterinary licenses in Kerala?
fields.explanation: The Kerala Veterinary Council regulates veterinary practice and issues licenses to practitioners in the state.

fields.text: What is the incubation period of Foot and Mouth Disease (FMD) in cattle?
fields.explanation: The incubation period for FMD in cattle ranges from 2 to 14 days, depending on the strain and exposure conditions.

fields.text: Which of the following is a common deworming drug for livestock?
fields.explanation: Albendazole is a broad-spectrum anthelmintic used for deworming livestock. The others are antibiotics, not dewormers.

fields.text: What is the primary symptom of Black Quarter disease in cattle?
fields.explanation: Black Quarter, caused by Clostridium chauvoei, is characterized by crepitating (crackling) swelling in muscles due to gas formation.

fields.text: Which breed of pig is commonly reared in India?
fields.explanation: Ghungroo is an indigenous Indian pig breed, valued for its adaptability. Yorkshire, Landrace, and Duroc are exotic breeds.

fields.text: What is the purpose of the National Animal Disease Control Programme (NADCP)?
fields.explanation: The NADCP aims to control Foot and Mouth Disease and Brucellosis through vaccination and surveillance in India.

fields.text: Which sample is used to diagnose Rabies in animals?
fields.explanation: Brain tissue is the sample of choice for diagnosing Rabies, typically via fluorescent antibody testing post-mortem.

fields.text: What is the ideal temperature range for brooding chicks?
fields.explanation: The ideal brooding temperature for chicks starts at 33–35°C for the first week, gradually decreasing as they grow.

fields.text: Which of the following is a common method of artificial insemination in cattle?
fields.explanation: Intrauterine insemination is the standard method for artificial insemination in cattle, delivering semen directly into the uterus.

fields.text: Which disease is also known as Hemorrhagic Septicemia in cattle?
fields.explanation: Hemorrhagic Septicemia, caused by Pasteurella multocida, is also known as Pasteurellosis, causing severe fever and swelling in cattle.

fields.text: Which of the following is an indigenous sheep breed of India?
fields.explanation: Deccani is an indigenous Indian sheep breed, known for its adaptability to arid regions. Merino, Dorset, and Suffolk are exotic breeds.

fields.text: What is the causative agent of Theileriosis in cattle?
fields.explanation: Theileriosis is caused by protozoa of the genus Theileria, transmitted by ticks, leading to fever and anemia in cattle.

fields.text: Which of the following is a synthetic breed of poultry developed in India?
fields.explanation: Giriraja is a synthetic poultry breed developed in India for dual-purpose (egg and meat) production. Aseel, Kadaknath, and Chittagong are indigenous breeds.

fields.text: What is the primary function of the Chief Disease Investigation Office (CDIO) in Kerala?
fields.explanation: The CDIO in Kerala focuses on diagnosing and monitoring livestock diseases to control outbreaks and ensure animal health.

fields.text: Which nutrient deficiency causes white muscle disease in calves?
fields.explanation: White muscle disease, or nutritional myopathy, is caused by selenium deficiency, leading to muscle degeneration in calves.

fields.text: What is the gestation period of a goat?
fields.explanation: The gestation period of a goat is approximately 150 days, varying slightly by breed and individual.

fields.text: Which of the following is a bacterial disease affecting goats?
fields.explanation: Johne's Disease, caused by Mycobacterium avium subsp. paratuberculosis, is a bacterial disease in goats. PPR, Orf, and Bluetongue are viral.

fields.text: What is the purpose of the Integrated Sample Survey (ISS) in animal husbandry?
fields.explanation: The ISS collects data to estimate livestock production, such as milk, meat, and eggs, for planning and policy-making.

fields.text: Which of the following is a common disinfectant used in veterinary hospitals?
fields.explanation: Formalin (formaldehyde solution) is a common disinfectant in veterinary hospitals for sterilizing equipment and surfaces. The others are medications.

fields.text: What is the primary vector for Babesiosis in cattle?
fields.explanation: Babesiosis, caused by Babesia parasites, is primarily transmitted by ticks, affecting cattle with fever and anemia.

fields.text: Which record must be maintained for livestock vaccination campaigns in Kerala?
fields.explanation: The Vaccination Register is mandatory to track livestock vaccinations, ensuring compliance with disease control programs.

fields.text: What is the main symptom of Marek's Disease in poultry?
fields.explanation: Marek's Disease, caused by a herpesvirus, primarily causes paralysis in poultry due to nerve damage.

fields.text: Which of the following is an exotic cattle breed?
fields.explanation: Holstein Friesian is an exotic cattle breed from the Netherlands, known for high milk yield. Tharparkar, Kankrej, and Ongole are indigenous.

fields.text: What is the ideal crude protein percentage in layer poultry feed?
fields.explanation: Layer poultry feed typically contains 16–18% crude protein to support egg production and hen health.

fields.text: Which disease is controlled by the Anthrax Spore Vaccine in cattle?
fields.explanation: The Anthrax Spore Vaccine is used to prevent Anthrax, a bacterial disease caused by Bacillus anthracis, in cattle.

fields.text: Which of the following is a common method for identifying livestock in India?
fields.explanation: Ear tagging is a widely used, cost-effective method for identifying livestock in India, allowing tracking for health and breeding purposes.

fields.text: What is the causative agent of Contagious Ecthyma in goats?
fields.explanation: Contagious Ecthyma, also known as Orf, is caused by a parapoxvirus, leading to skin lesions in goats and sheep.

fields.text: Which of the following is a dual-purpose cattle breed in India?
fields.explanation: Hariana is an indigenous Indian cattle breed used for both milk production and draught purposes. The others are exotic dairy breeds.

fields.text: What is the primary objective of the Rashtriya Gokul Mission?
fields.explanation: The Rashtriya Gokul Mission aims to conserve and develop indigenous cattle breeds to enhance milk production and genetic diversity.

fields.text: Which parasite causes mange in livestock?
fields.explanation: Mange is caused by mites, such as Sarcoptes or Psoroptes, leading to skin irritation and hair loss in livestock.

fields.text: Which officer is responsible for maintaining the stock register in a veterinary hospital?
fields.explanation: The clerk in a veterinary hospital is typically responsible for maintaining the stock register, tracking medicines and supplies.

fields.text: What is the incubation period of Peste des Petits Ruminants (PPR) in goats?
fields.explanation: PPR, caused by a morbillivirus, has an incubation period of 2–6 days in goats, leading to fever and respiratory symptoms.

fields.text: Which of the following is a symptom of Fowl Pox in poultry?
fields.explanation: Fowl Pox, caused by a poxvirus, causes scabby skin lesions on unfeathered areas like the comb and wattles in poultry.

fields.text: What is the main source of calcium in livestock feed?
fields.explanation: Limestone is a common calcium supplement in livestock feed, essential for bone development and eggshell formation.

fields.text: Which disease is caused by Clostridium perfringens in sheep?
fields.explanation: Enterotoxemia, or overeating disease, is caused by Clostridium perfringens, producing toxins that affect sheep, especially lambs.

fields.text: What is the purpose of the Animal Husbandry Department's mobile veterinary units in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Mobile veterinary units provide on-site treatment and vaccination services in remote areas, improving livestock healthcare access.

fields.text: Which of the following is a viral disease of pigs?
fields.explanation: Swine Fever, or Classical Swine Fever, is caused by a pestivirus, affecting pigs with fever and hemorrhages. The others are bacterial.

fields.text: What is the ideal floor space requirement per bird in a deep litter poultry system?
fields.explanation: In a deep litter system, each poultry bird requires 1–2 square feet of floor space for optimal growth and comfort.

fields.text: Which of the following is a non-infectious disease in cattle?
fields.explanation: Ketosis is a metabolic disorder in cattle caused by energy imbalance, often post-calving. The others are infectious diseases.

fields.text: Which document is required for the interstate transport of livestock in India?
fields.explanation: A Health Certificate, issued by a veterinary officer, is required to ensure livestock are disease-free during interstate transport.

fields.text: Which of the following is a common breed of duck reared in India?
fields.explanation: Indian Runner is a popular duck breed in India, known for its upright posture and egg-laying capacity. Muscovy and Pekin are exotic, while Mallard is wild.

fields.text: What is the causative agent of Avian Leucosis in poultry?
fields.explanation: Avian Leucosis is caused by retroviruses, leading to tumors and immunosuppression in poultry.

fields.text: Which of the following is an indigenous cattle breed of Kerala?
fields.explanation: Vechur is a dwarf indigenous cattle breed of Kerala, known for its small size and high milk fat content. The others are exotic breeds.

fields.text: What is the primary goal of the National Dairy Development Board (NDDB)?
fields.explanation: The NDDB aims to promote dairy development through cooperatives, enhancing milk production and farmer livelihoods.

fields.text: Which of the following is a symptom of Coccidiosis in poultry?
fields.explanation: Coccidiosis, caused by Eimeria protozoa, causes bloody diarrhea in poultry due to intestinal damage.

fields.text: Who is responsible for conducting livestock census in India?
fields.explanation: The Animal Husbandry Department, under the Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry, and Dairying, conducts the livestock census every five years.

fields.text: What is the gestation period of a pig?
fields.explanation: The gestation period of a pig is approximately 114 days (about 3 months, 3 weeks, and 3 days).

fields.text: Which of the following is a fungal disease in livestock?
fields.explanation: Ringworm, caused by fungi like Trichophyton, affects livestock skin. The others are caused by bacteria or protozoa.

fields.text: What is the purpose of the Kerala Veterinary and Animal Sciences University (KVASU)?
fields.explanation: KVASU focuses on veterinary education, research, and extension services to improve animal health and productivity.

fields.text: Which vaccine is used to prevent Newcastle Disease in poultry?
fields.explanation: The Lasota strain vaccine is used to prevent Newcastle Disease, protecting poultry from this viral infection.

fields.text: What is the main component of silage used in livestock feeding?
fields.explanation: Silage is made from fermented green fodder, preserving nutrients for livestock feeding during dry seasons.

fields.text: Which of the following is a zoonotic disease affecting pigs?
fields.explanation: Leptospirosis, caused by Leptospira bacteria, is a zoonotic disease transmissible from pigs to humans through contact with infected urine.

fields.text: What is the ideal humidity range for a poultry house?
fields.explanation: A humidity range of 40–60% is ideal for poultry houses to prevent respiratory issues and maintain bird health.

fields.text: Which of the following is a non-contagious disease in goats?
fields.explanation: Bloat is a non-contagious condition caused by gas accumulation in the rumen. The others are infectious diseases.

fields.text: What is the primary purpose of the Livestock Health and Disease Control Programme (LHDCP)?
fields.explanation: The LHDCP aims to control major animal diseases through vaccination, surveillance, and diagnostic services.

fields.text: Providing food grains to the poorest of the poor, the central government passed a social welfare scheme on 25th December 2000 called?
fields.explanation: Antodaya Anna Yojana, launched on 25th December 2000, provides subsidized food grains to the poorest households in India to ensure food security.

fields.text: The Right to Information (RTI) Act 2005 came into full force on?
fields.explanation: The RTI Act 2005, enabling citizens to access government information, came into full force on 12th October 2005.

fields.text: The martyr of the Paliyam Satyagraha was?
fields.explanation: A.G. Velayudhan was a martyr of the Paliyam Satyagraha (1947–48), a movement in Kerala for temple entry and against untouchability.

fields.text: The author of 'Jathi Kummi' was?
fields.explanation: Pandit Karuppan, a social reformer, wrote 'Jathi Kummi,' a poem criticizing caste discrimination in Kerala society.

fields.text: Fundamental rights guaranteed by the Indian Constitution can be suspended only by?

options.A: An Act passed by the parliament
options.C: The judicial decision of the Supreme Court

fields.explanation: Under Article 359, fundamental rights can be suspended during a national emergency proclaimed by the President of India.

fields.text: Who conducted 'Adi Lahala for Dalith'?
fields.explanation: Poikayil Yohannan, also known as Kumara Guru, led the 'Adi Lahala' movement for Dalit rights and against caste oppression in Kerala.

fields.text: Right to Information is?

options.D: Both Fundamental as well as Legal Right

fields.explanation: The Right to Information is a legal right under the RTI Act 2005, not a fundamental right under the Indian Constitution.

fields.text: 'Ente Jeevitha Smaranakal' is the autobiography of?
fields.explanation: 'Ente Jeevitha Smaranakal' is the autobiography of Mannathu Padmanabhan, a prominent social reformer and founder of Nair Service Society.

fields.text: The first social agitation in Kerala was?
fields.explanation: The Channar Agitation (1813–1859) was the first social agitation in Kerala, where lower-caste Channar women fought for the right to cover their upper bodies.

fields.text: According to the Constitution, the Lok Sabha must meet at least?

options.A: Thrice each year with no more than two months between sessions
options.B: Thrice each year with no more than four months between sessions
options.C: Twice each year with no more than six months between sessions
options.D: Twice each year with no more than four months between sessions

fields.explanation: Under Article 85, the Lok Sabha must meet at least twice a year, with no more than six months between sessions, to ensure regular parliamentary functioning.

fields.text: The plan introduced by the state government to prevent sexual attacks against children and women in 2014 was?
fields.explanation: The Nirbhaya scheme, launched in 2014 by the Kerala government, aims to prevent sexual violence against women and children through awareness and support systems.

fields.text: A national-level campaign was introduced to clean the streets, roads, and infrastructure of the country called?
fields.explanation: Swachh Bharat Abhiyan, launched in 2014, is a national campaign to clean India's streets, roads, and infrastructure, promoting sanitation and hygiene.

fields.text: Who determines the salaries of the High Court?
fields.explanation: The salaries of High Court judges are determined by the Parliament of India, as per the Constitution, ensuring uniformity across states.

fields.text: Which was the first school established by the government to impart English education in Travancore?
fields.explanation: Rajas Free School, established in 1834 in Travancore, was the first government school to provide English education in the region.

fields.text: The social reformer who accepted the title of Bharath Kesari from Dr. Rajendra Prasad was?
fields.explanation: Mannath Padmanabhan, a prominent Kerala social reformer and founder of Nair Service Society, was honored with the title Bharath Kesari by Dr. Rajendra Prasad for his contributions to social upliftment.

fields.text: The founder of the printing press in Kerala was?
fields.explanation: Benjamin Bailey, a missionary, established the first printing press in Kerala in 1821 at Kottayam, significantly advancing education and literature.

fields.text: Kodungallor Kunhikuttan Thampuran is commonly known as?
fields.explanation: Kodungallor Kunhikuttan Thampuran, a prolific Malayalam poet and translator, is known as Kerala Vyasa for his literary contributions, including translating the Mahabharata.

fields.text: The Nobel Prize in Literature 2016 was awarded to?
fields.explanation: Bob Dylan, an American singer-songwriter, received the 2016 Nobel Prize in Literature for his poetic expressions in music.

fields.text: 'Mission Indradhanush' launched by the Government of India is for?

options.B: Immunization of children and pregnant women
options.C: Construction of smart cities across the country

fields.explanation: Mission Indradhanush, launched in 2014, aims to immunize children and pregnant women against preventable diseases through a targeted vaccination campaign.

fields.text: Which among these is also known as Bang disease?
fields.explanation: Brucellosis, caused by Brucella bacteria, is also known as Bang disease, affecting livestock and causing reproductive issues like abortions.

fields.text: Termination code for protein synthesis is?
fields.explanation: UGA is a stop codon in mRNA, signaling the termination of protein synthesis during translation in the ribosome.

fields.text: Diploid chromosome number in Sus scrofa is?
fields.explanation: Sus scrofa (domestic pig) has a diploid chromosome number of 38, as established in cytogenetic studies of swine.

fields.text: In crossing between Rose combed and Pea combed varieties, in F2 generation Walnut, Rose, Pea, and Single combed varieties appear in the proportion of?
fields.explanation: In poultry, crossing Rose and Pea combed varieties follows Mendelian genetics, producing Walnut, Rose, Pea, and Single combs in a 9:3:3:1 ratio in the F2 generation due to two interacting genes.

fields.text: Plaster of Paris is the powder of?
fields.explanation: Plaster of Paris is made from calcium sulphate hemihydrate, used in veterinary casts for immobilizing fractures.

fields.text: Total abnormality of spermatozoa in semen sample should not exceed?
fields.explanation: In livestock semen analysis, total sperm abnormalities should not exceed 20% to ensure fertility, as higher levels reduce reproductive success.

fields.text: Number of ribs present in equines is?
fields.explanation: Equines (horses) have 18 pairs of ribs, a key anatomical feature distinguishing them from other livestock species.

fields.text: A sexually deformed female partner of a heterosexual twin is called?
fields.explanation: A freemartin is a sexually deformed female calf born as a twin to a male, caused by hormonal exchange in utero, leading to infertility.

fields.text: Milk vein in cattle is?
fields.explanation: The milk vein in cattle is the subcutaneous abdominal vein (epigastric vein), visible in high-yielding dairy cows due to increased blood flow to the udder.

fields.text: The term frog is associated in the hoof of?
fields.explanation: The frog is a V-shaped, shock-absorbing structure in the hoof of a horse, aiding in traction and circulation.

fields.text: Hageman factor is?
fields.explanation: Hageman factor, or Factor XII, is a clotting factor in the blood coagulation cascade, initiating the intrinsic pathway.

fields.text: Ovulation generally occurs in response to?
fields.explanation: Ovulation in mammals is triggered by a surge in Luteinizing Hormone (LH), which stimulates the release of the egg from the ovary.

fields.text: Primary oocytes are?
fields.explanation: Primary oocytes are diploid cells in the ovary, containing two sets of chromosomes before undergoing meiosis to form mature eggs.

fields.text: Which antibiotic causes Vitamin K deficiency?
fields.explanation: Sulphaquinoxalin, a sulfonamide antibiotic, can disrupt gut bacteria that produce Vitamin K, leading to deficiency in animals.

fields.text: Humoral immunity is mediated by circulating antibodies produced by?
fields.explanation: Humoral immunity involves antibodies produced by plasma cells, derived from B lymphocytes, which target pathogens in body fluids.

fields.text: Anaesthetic contraindicated in cats is?
fields.explanation: Amobarbital, a barbiturate, is contraindicated in cats due to their sensitivity to barbiturates, which can cause prolonged recovery or toxicity.

fields.text: Which is the principal prehensile organ in equines?
fields.explanation: The tongue is the primary prehensile organ in equines, used to grasp and manipulate food during grazing or feeding.

fields.text: The growth promoter in calf thymus extract is?
fields.explanation: Promine, a component in calf thymus extract, is identified as a growth-promoting factor in veterinary applications.

fields.text: Digestive enzyme of pancreas is known as?
fields.explanation: Zymogen refers to inactive digestive enzyme precursors (e.g., trypsinogen) produced by the pancreas, activated in the digestive tract.

fields.text: Lactation tetany in mare is caused by?
fields.explanation: Lactation tetany in mares, also known as eclampsia, is caused by hypocalcemia (low calcium levels), often during early lactation.

fields.text: Direction of DNA synthesis is?
fields.explanation: DNA synthesis occurs in the 5' to 3' direction, as DNA polymerase adds nucleotides to the 3' end of the growing strand.

fields.text: Bile serves as an important excretory route for?
fields.explanation: Bile excretes cholesterol from the liver into the digestive tract, aiding in fat digestion and waste elimination.

fields.text: Which among these is not present in DNA?
fields.explanation: Uracil is found in RNA, not DNA, which contains thymine as one of its nitrogenous bases alongside adenine, cytosine, and guanine.

fields.text: Aginomoto is chemically a?
fields.explanation: Aginomoto, a flavor enhancer used in animal feed and food, is chemically monosodium glutamate, an amino acid salt.

fields.text: Vitamin C is abundantly available in?
fields.explanation: Gooseberry (Indian gooseberry or amla) is a rich source of Vitamin C, widely used in animal feed supplements to boost immunity.

fields.text: The special feeding before and during the mating season is?
fields.explanation: Flushing is the practice of providing extra nutrition to female livestock before and during mating to improve ovulation and conception rates.

fields.text: The act of mating in sheep is called?
fields.explanation: Tupping refers to the mating process in sheep, where a ram (tup) mates with ewes during the breeding season.

fields.text: The age at which quails start laying is?
fields.explanation: Quails typically begin laying eggs at 6–8 weeks of age under optimal conditions, making them a fast-maturing poultry species.

fields.text: Mandya is a breed of?
fields.explanation: Mandya is an indigenous Indian sheep breed, known for its meat quality and adaptability to South Indian conditions.

fields.text: The maximum permissible limit of fluorine in drinking water is?
fields.explanation: The maximum permissible limit of fluorine in drinking water for livestock is 1.5 ppm to prevent fluorosis, which affects bones and teeth.

fields.text: The amino acid which has influence on egg weight is?
fields.explanation: Methionine, an essential amino acid, influences egg weight in poultry by supporting protein synthesis and eggshell formation.

fields.text: The average weaning weight of Landrace piglet is?
fields.explanation: Landrace piglets typically have an average weaning weight of around 8 kg at 3–4 weeks, depending on management practices.

fields.text: Water content in cow's milk is?
fields.explanation: Cow's milk contains approximately 87% water, contributing to its nutritional and fluid properties for calves and human consumption.

fields.text: Major protein of chicken egg white is?
fields.explanation: Albumin (ovalbumin) is the major protein in chicken egg white, constituting about 54% of its protein content, essential for embryo nutrition.

fields.text: Quails belong to the family?
fields.explanation: Quails belong to the Phasianidae family, which includes pheasants, partridges, and other gallinaceous birds, commonly raised for eggs and meat.

fields.text: The genes carried on X chromosomes are called?
fields.explanation: Sex-linked genes are carried on the X chromosome, affecting traits like color blindness or certain poultry traits, inherited differently in males and females.

fields.text: Molecular scissors are the name given to?
fields.explanation: Restriction endonucleases, used in genetic engineering, are called molecular scissors for their ability to cut DNA at specific recognition sites.

fields.text: Mule is a cross obtained from?
fields.explanation: A mule is a hybrid from a male ass (donkey) and a female horse (mare), known for its strength and sterility.

fields.text: Genes controlling sex-influenced traits are located on?
fields.explanation: Sex-influenced traits, like horn development in cattle, are controlled by genes on autosomes but expressed differently based on sex hormones.

fields.text: The number of linkage groups in an autotetraploid with 48 chromosomes is?
fields.explanation: In an autotetraploid with 48 chromosomes (4n), the number of linkage groups equals the haploid number (n). Since 48/4 = 12, there are 12 linkage groups.

fields.text: Panmixia is an alternate term for?
fields.explanation: Panmixia refers to random mating in a population, where individuals mate without regard to genetic traits, maintaining genetic diversity.

fields.text: Buffalo with sickle-shaped horns is?
fields.explanation: Surti buffalo, an Indian breed, is characterized by sickle-shaped horns, known for high milk yield and adaptability.

fields.text: Diagnostic test for mastitis is?
fields.explanation: The California Mastitis Test (CMT) is a widely used diagnostic test to detect mastitis in dairy animals by identifying somatic cell counts in milk.

fields.text: Average daily feed intake of a laying hen is?
fields.explanation: A laying hen consumes approximately 110 grams of feed daily to support egg production and maintenance under standard conditions.

fields.text: Amino acid absorption in ruminants takes place mainly in?
fields.explanation: In ruminants, amino acids are primarily absorbed in the small intestine, where digested proteins are broken down into absorbable forms.

fields.text: Example for ketogenic amino acid is?
fields.explanation: Leucine is a ketogenic amino acid, metabolized into ketone bodies, providing energy in certain physiological states.

fields.text: Which of the following is the causative agent of Foot and Mouth Disease?
fields.explanation: Foot and Mouth Disease is caused by an RNA virus belonging to the family Picornaviridae, genus Aphthovirus.

fields.text: The normal gestation period of a cow is approximately?
fields.explanation: The normal gestation period of cattle is approximately 280-285 days (about 9 months and 10 days).

fields.text: Which vitamin deficiency causes Night Blindness in animals?
fields.explanation: Vitamin A deficiency leads to night blindness as it is essential for rhodopsin formation in the retina.

fields.text: The scientific name of Indian Water Buffalo is?
fields.explanation: The scientific name of Indian Water Buffalo is Bubalus bubalis, while Bos taurus and Bos indicus refer to cattle species.

fields.text: Which hormone is responsible for milk ejection in dairy animals?
fields.explanation: Oxytocin is the hormone responsible for milk ejection reflex, causing contraction of mammary gland muscles.

fields.text: The most common method of artificial insemination in cattle uses semen stored in?
fields.explanation: Cattle semen is typically stored in liquid nitrogen at -196°C for long-term preservation and viability.

fields.text: The normal body temperature of cattle ranges between?
fields.explanation: The normal body temperature of cattle ranges from 101-103°F (38.3-39.4°C).

fields.text: Which nutrient is most important for bone development in young animals?
fields.explanation: Calcium and Phosphorus are essential minerals for proper bone formation and development in young animals.

fields.text: The disease caused by Clostridium tetani is?
fields.explanation: Clostridium tetani causes tetanus, characterized by muscle spasms and rigidity due to tetanospasmin toxin.

fields.text: Which breed of cattle is known for highest milk production?
fields.explanation: Holstein Friesian is known for the highest milk production among dairy breeds, though with lower fat content.

fields.text: The term 'Colostrum' refers to?
fields.explanation: Colostrum is the first milk produced after parturition, rich in antibodies and essential for newborn immunity.

fields.text: Which parasite causes Fascioliasis in cattle?
fields.explanation: Fascioliasis is caused by liver flukes (Fasciola hepatica and F. gigantica) affecting the liver and bile ducts.

fields.text: The optimum pH of rumen contents in healthy cattle is?
fields.explanation: The optimum pH of rumen contents in healthy cattle ranges from 6.0-7.0 for proper microbial fermentation.

fields.text: Which vaccine is used to prevent Rinderpest in cattle?
fields.explanation: Rinderpest was officially eradicated globally in 2011, so vaccination is no longer needed or practiced.

fields.text: The estrous cycle length in cattle is approximately?
fields.explanation: The estrous cycle in cattle averages 21 days (range 18-24 days) from one estrus to the next.

fields.text: Which mineral deficiency causes Goiter in animals?
fields.explanation: Iodine deficiency causes goiter (enlarged thyroid gland) as iodine is essential for thyroid hormone synthesis.

fields.text: The causative agent of Tuberculosis in cattle is?
fields.explanation: Mycobacterium bovis is the primary causative agent of tuberculosis in cattle and other animals.

fields.text: Which part of the digestive system is absent in poultry?
fields.explanation: Poultry lack a rumen as they are monogastric animals, unlike ruminants such as cattle, sheep, and goats.

fields.text: The normal heart rate of cattle per minute is?
fields.explanation: The normal heart rate of adult cattle ranges from 60-80 beats per minute.

fields.text: Which disease is also known as 'Mad Cow Disease'?
fields.explanation: BSE (Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy) is commonly known as 'Mad Cow Disease', a prion disease affecting the brain.

fields.text: The practice of feeding animals according to their production level is called?
fields.explanation: Challenge feeding involves providing extra nutrition above maintenance requirements to support higher production levels.

fields.text: Which vitamin is synthesized by rumen microorganisms?
fields.explanation: Vitamin K is synthesized by rumen microorganisms, along with most B-complex vitamins in ruminants.

fields.text: The term 'Dry period' in dairy cattle refers to?

options.B: Period between calving and first estrus
options.C: Period when cow is not milked before next calving

fields.explanation: Dry period is the time when a pregnant cow is not milked, typically 6-8 weeks before the next calving.

fields.text: Which antibody provides passive immunity to newborn calves through colostrum?
fields.explanation: IgG is the predominant antibody in bovine colostrum that provides passive immunity to newborn calves.

fields.text: The scientific name of domestic goat is?
fields.explanation: The scientific name of domestic goat is Capra hircus, while Ovis aries refers to domestic sheep.

fields.text: Which hormone controls ovulation in farm animals?
fields.explanation: Luteinizing Hormone (LH) triggers ovulation in female farm animals through its surge mechanism.

fields.text: The most common cause of mastitis in dairy cattle is?
fields.explanation: Staphylococcus aureus is one of the most common contagious pathogens causing clinical and subclinical mastitis in dairy cattle.

fields.text: Which test is used to detect subclinical mastitis?
fields.explanation: California Mastitis Test (CMT) is widely used to detect subclinical mastitis by measuring somatic cell count.

fields.text: The gestation period of sheep is approximately?
fields.explanation: The gestation period of sheep is approximately 150 days (about 5 months).

fields.text: Which nutrient provides the highest energy per unit weight?
fields.explanation: Fats provide approximately 9 kcal/g, which is more than double the energy provided by carbohydrates and proteins (4 kcal/g each).

fields.text: The causative agent of Contagious Bovine Pleuropneumonia is?
fields.explanation: Contagious Bovine Pleuropneumonia is caused by Mycoplasma mycoides subsp. mycoides, a cell wall-deficient bacterium.

fields.text: Which method is used for pregnancy diagnosis in large animals?
fields.explanation: All these methods - rectal palpation, blood tests (pregnancy-specific proteins), and ultrasound - are used for pregnancy diagnosis.

fields.text: The normal respiratory rate of cattle per minute is?
fields.explanation: The normal respiratory rate of adult cattle ranges from 20-30 breaths per minute.

fields.text: Which disease is caused by Brucella abortus in cattle?
fields.explanation: Brucella abortus causes brucellosis in cattle, leading to abortion, infertility, and decreased milk production.

fields.text: The feed conversion ratio in poultry is typically?
fields.explanation: Modern broiler chickens have excellent feed conversion ratios of 1.5:1 to 2:1, meaning they require 1.5-2 kg feed per kg body weight gain.

fields.text: Which amino acid is considered the first limiting amino acid in poultry diets?
fields.explanation: Methionine is typically the first limiting amino acid in practical poultry diets, especially in corn-soybean meal based diets.

fields.text: The causative agent of Newcastle Disease in poultry is?
fields.explanation: Newcastle Disease is caused by Newcastle Disease Virus (NDV), an avian paramyxovirus affecting poultry.

fields.text: Which organ produces insulin in animals?
fields.explanation: Insulin is produced by beta cells in the islets of Langerhans in the pancreas, regulating blood glucose levels.

fields.text: The recommended protein percentage in dairy cattle concentrate feed is?
fields.explanation: Dairy cattle concentrate feeds typically contain 16-18% crude protein to support milk production requirements.

fields.text: Which disease is characterized by diamond-shaped skin lesions in pigs?
fields.explanation: Swine Erysipelas, caused by Erysipelothrix rhusiopathiae, is characterized by diamond-shaped skin lesions in pigs.

fields.text: The normal gestation period of pigs is approximately?
fields.explanation: The gestation period of pigs is approximately 114 days (3 months, 3 weeks, and 3 days).

fields.text: The term 'Artificial Insemination' was first coined by?
fields.explanation: Lazzaro Spallanzani, an Italian scientist, first successfully performed artificial insemination in dogs in 1784.

fields.text: Which hormone is responsible for mammary gland development?
fields.explanation: Mammary gland development requires the coordinated action of estrogen, progesterone, and growth hormone.

fields.text: The most common method of castration in calves is?
fields.explanation: Surgical castration is the most common and effective method for castrating young calves, though elastrator bands are also widely used.

fields.text: Which parasitic disease is also known as 'Surra' in animals?
fields.explanation: Trypanosomiasis, caused by Trypanosoma evansi, is commonly known as 'Surra' and affects various domestic animals.

fields.text: The optimum temperature for incubating chicken eggs is?
fields.explanation: The optimum temperature for incubating chicken eggs is 99.5°F (37.5°C) with 60-65% relative humidity.

fields.text: Which breed of buffalo is known as the 'Black Gold of Punjab'?
fields.explanation: Murrah buffalo, originating from Haryana and Punjab, is known as the 'Black Gold of Punjab' due to its excellent milk production.

fields.text: The science of animal breeding is also known as?
fields.explanation: Zootechnics is the science of animal breeding and management, encompassing genetics, nutrition, reproduction, and husbandry practices.

fields.text: The Article in the Constitution which gives the Primary Education in Mother Tongue:
fields.explanation: Article 350(A) of the Indian Constitution mandates facilities for instruction in the mother tongue at the primary education stage.

fields.text: On whose recommendation was the Constituent Assembly formed?
fields.explanation: The Constituent Assembly was formed based on the recommendations of the Cabinet Mission Plan of 1946.

fields.text: The first state to become bifurcated after Independence was
fields.explanation: Bombay was bifurcated into Maharashtra and Gujarat in 1960, making it the first state to be split after Independence.

fields.text: In which state of India the famous festival of Horn bill celebrated?
fields.explanation: The Hornbill Festival, a cultural event, is celebrated annually in Nagaland.

fields.text: First Modern factory for the manufacture of coir was opened at Alleppey during the period of
fields.explanation: The first modern coir factory was established in Alleppey during Ayilyam Thirunal's reign.

fields.text: Who says the Revolutionary message 'No Caste, No Religion, No God for Man'?
fields.explanation: Sahodaran Ayyappan advocated the revolutionary message promoting equality and rationality.

fields.text: First Chief Minister of Kerala from a Backward Community was
fields.explanation: C.H. Muhammed Koya was the first Chief Minister from a backward community in Kerala.

fields.text: The Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act (PWDVA) came into force on
fields.explanation: The PWDVA was enforced on 26th October 2006 to protect women from domestic violence.

fields.text: Name the First women Magazine published in Kerala.
fields.explanation: Vidhya Vinodini was the first women's magazine published in Kerala, focusing on women's issues.

fields.text: _______ was the Thakazhi Sivasankaran Pillai's work.
fields.explanation: Enippadikal is a renowned literary work by Thakazhi Sivasankara Pillai.

fields.text: Which of the following the Iron lady of Kerala?
fields.explanation: Akkamma Cheriyan, a freedom fighter, is known as the Iron Lady of Kerala.

fields.text: The Historic site 'Hampi' is located in which of the following states:
fields.explanation: Hampi, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is located in Karnataka.

fields.text: Which of the following was the leader who not participated of Nivarthana Agitation?
fields.explanation: G.K Pillai did not participate in the Nivarthana Agitation, a significant social movement in Kerala.

fields.text: The first session of the Praja mandal be held at _______ in January 1942.
fields.explanation: The Praja Mandal's first session was held in Cochin in January 1942.

fields.text: During inflation the value of currency
fields.explanation: Inflation reduces the purchasing power of currency, causing its value to decrease.

fields.text: Overvaluation of closing stock at the time of preparing P & L A/c will result in _______ statement of reported profit.
fields.explanation: Overvaluing closing stock inflates the reported profit in the Profit & Loss Account.

fields.text: Salaries outstanding of the previous year which is paid during the year is _______ to the current year.
fields.explanation: Outstanding salaries paid in the current year are a payment liability for the current year.

fields.text: An error in accounting transaction which cannot be detected by checking the trial balance as its effect is neutralised by another error, is called:
fields.explanation: Compensating errors cancel each other out, making them undetectable in the trial balance.

fields.text: A type of goodwill which is directly relate to reputation in the name of the institution is called:
fields.explanation: Cat goodwill is associated with the reputation of an institution's name.

fields.text: The fixed element of working capital is called:
fields.explanation: Hard core working capital is the fixed portion required for permanent business needs.

fields.text: Expenses due but not paid is a _______ account.
fields.explanation: Expenses due but not paid are recorded as a personal account, representing a liability.

fields.text: Convention of consistency states that:

options.A: All possible losses should be taken into account on accrual basis.
options.B: Same accounting principles should be followed over a series of years to make the accounts comparable.
options.C: All possible profits should be taken into account on accrual basis.
options.D: All possible losses and should be taken into account on accrual basis.

fields.explanation: The convention of consistency ensures comparability by using the same accounting principles over time.

fields.text: The ability of a concern to meet its long term liabilities as and when it becomes due is called:
fields.explanation: Solvency is the ability to meet long-term liabilities as they become due.

fields.text: Which is a capital expenditure?
fields.explanation: All listed options are capital expenditures, providing long-term benefits.

fields.text: Under _______ method of valuation of shares, from the market value of assets the amount of external liabilities and preference share capital is deducted and the result is divided by the number of equity shares.
fields.explanation: The Net Asset Value method calculates share value by deducting liabilities and preference capital from asset value.

fields.text: An extra commission paid by the consignor to the consignee for guaranteeing credit sales is called
fields.explanation: Del credere commission is paid for guaranteeing the collection of credit sales.

fields.text: The sequence of accounting procedures repeated each accounting year to record and summarise business transactions is called
fields.explanation: The Accounting Cycle is the repeated process of recording and summarizing transactions.

fields.text: Assets exhausted in the process of earning revenue are called
fields.explanation: Wasting assets, like mines, are depleted in the process of earning revenue.

fields.text: The customs and traditions followed by the accountants for the preparation of the financial statements:
fields.explanation: Accounting conventions are the customs followed in financial statement preparation.

fields.text: A situation when a firm gets a profit lower than the average profit of the industry is called:
fields.explanation: Overcapitalisation occurs when profits are below the industry average due to excess capital.

fields.text: The trial balance is prepared either by _______ or by _______ method.
fields.explanation: Trial balance is prepared using the balance or total method.

fields.text: A debit note is prepared by the _______ of goods when he returns some of the goods purchased.
fields.explanation: The purchaser prepares a debit note when returning goods.

fields.text: Trade discount is recorded:

options.C: Both in the P&L A/c and Balance sheet

fields.explanation: Trade discount is not recorded in accounts; it is deducted from the invoice.

fields.text: _______ is the expenditure of a revenue nature, the benefit of which lasts for more than one accounting year.
fields.explanation: Deferred Revenue Expenditure benefits multiple accounting years.

fields.text: That part of subscribed capital set apart to be called only at the time of liquidation of a company is called:
fields.explanation: Reserve capital is set aside for use only during company liquidation.

fields.text: The type of depreciation by which the value of an asset is reduced due of change in technology or fashion is called
fields.explanation: Obsolescence is depreciation due to technological or fashion changes.

fields.text: _______ is the amount received by non-profit making organisations on the death of a person as per his will.
fields.explanation: A legacy is an amount received by non-profits as per a will.

fields.text: _______ is the amount of profit earned by a concern over and above the normal return expected on its investment.
fields.explanation: Super profit is the excess profit above normal returns.

fields.text: Calls-in-advance is a/an _______ to a company.
fields.explanation: Calls-in-advance are a liability, as they represent funds received before due.

fields.text: Kerala Co-operative Milk Marketing Federation (KCMMF), popularly called 'MILMA' was established in with its head office at Trivandrum for the successful implementation of 'Operation Flood' Programme in Kerala.
fields.explanation: MILMA was established in April 1980 to implement Operation Flood in Kerala.

fields.text: The founded in 1844, is usually considered the first successful co-operative enterprise, used as a model for modern coops.
fields.explanation: The Rochdale Society, founded in 1844, set the model for modern cooperatives.

fields.text: Expand CICED, which was established in order to enhance education quality in developing countries.

options.A: Centre for Indian Co-operative Education and Deployment
options.B: Centre for International Co-operation in Education Development
options.C: Commissioner of Indian Co-operative Education Department
options.D: Consortium for International Co-operation in Education Development

fields.explanation: CICED stands for Centre for International Co-operation in Education Development.

fields.text: The first Scheduled Bank in the Co-operative sector was the
fields.explanation: The Kerala State Co-operative Bank (KSCB) was the first scheduled bank in the cooperative sector.

fields.text: The was the first international association formed (1895) by the cooperative movement which includes the World Council of Credit Unions.
fields.explanation: The International Co-operative Alliance, formed in 1895, was the first international cooperative association.

fields.text: Who was a social reformer and pioneer of the cooperative movement?
fields.explanation: Robert Owen was a key pioneer of the cooperative movement and social reformer.

fields.text: A is a cooperative that is run by and for a network of volunteers, for the benefit of a defined membership or the general public, to achieve some goal.
fields.explanation: A volunteer cooperative is run by volunteers for a defined membership or public benefit.

fields.text: Co-operative flag was designed by
fields.explanation: The International Co-operative Alliance (ICA) designed the cooperative flag.

fields.text: National Agricultural Co-operative Marketing Federation of India Ltd. (NAFED) was established on the auspicious day of Gandhi Jayanti on
fields.explanation: NAFED was established on 2nd October 1958, on Gandhi Jayanti.

fields.text: Cooperative Development Services was founded in 1985 by
fields.explanation: Rod Nilsestuen founded Cooperative Development Services in 1985.

fields.text: Who undertakes the responsibility of providing Co-operative Education and Training in Kerala?
fields.explanation: The State Co-operative Union in Kerala provides cooperative education and training.

fields.text: "Circle Co-operative Union" means a circle co-operative union constituted under Section of the Kerala Co-operative Societies Act.
fields.explanation: Circle Co-operative Union is constituted under Section 88 of the Kerala Co-operative Societies Act.

fields.text: means a society having jurisdiction over one or more Revenue Districts but not the whole of the State as its area of operation; and having as its members only other societies and declared as such by the Registrar or the Government.
fields.explanation: A Central society operates over one or more revenue districts with only societies as members.

fields.text: " member" means a member who possesses only such privileges and rights of a member and who is subject only to such liabilities of a member as may be specified in the bye-laws.

options.D: Member by estoppel or holding out

fields.explanation: Nominal or associate members have limited privileges as per the bye-laws.

fields.text: The number of applicants for registration of a society shall not be less than

options.A: Ten, each of such person being a member of a different family.
options.B: Twenty-five, each of such person being a member of same family.
options.C: Ten each of such person being a member of same family.
options.D: Twenty-five, each of such person being a member of a different family.

fields.explanation: Registration requires at least twenty-five applicants from different families.

fields.text: The following one is not a reason for the Cancellation of registration certificates of societies:

options.A: Where the whole of the assets and liabilities of a society are transferred to another society in accordance with the provisions of section-14.
options.B: Where two or more societies are amalgamated into a new society in accordance with the provisions of Section-14
options.C: Where a society is divided into two or more societies in accordance with the provisions of Section-14.

fields.explanation: All listed options are valid reasons for cancellation under Section 14.

fields.text: Every member of a society shall exercise his vote:
fields.explanation: Members must vote in person; proxy voting is not allowed in cooperatives.

fields.text: Co-operative Arbitration Court refers to a court formed under Section of the Act.
fields.explanation: The Co-operative Arbitration Court is formed under Section 70A.

fields.text: The committee of a society may, at any time, call a special general body meeting of the society and shall call such meeting within one month:

options.B: after receipt of a requisition in writing from the Registrar
options.C: a proportion of the total number of members, as may be prescribed.

fields.explanation: A special meeting can be called based on members, Registrar, or prescribed proportion.

fields.text: The Government may provide moneys to a society for the purchase of shares in other societies with limited liability. It is

options.A: Direct partnership of Government in societies
options.B: Indirect partnership of Government in societies

fields.explanation: Providing funds for shares is an indirect government partnership.

fields.text: The following one is not true in respect of A society: It shall, out of its net profits in any year,

options.A: Transfer an amount not being less than fifteen per cent of the net profits to the reserve fund;
options.B: Credit such portion of the net profits, not exceeding five per cent, as may be prescribed, to the Co-operative Education Fund referred to in clause (xix) of subsection (2) of Section 109.
options.C: Pay, in the case of a Central Society, including a District Cooperative Bank, dividend to its members, on their paid up share capital such rate not exceeding twenty per cent.

fields.explanation: All options are true regarding profit allocation in a cooperative society.

fields.text: The shall by notification in the official gazette, appoint a person to be the Director of Co-operative Audit with jurisdiction over the whole of the State.
fields.explanation: The Government appoints the Director of Co-operative Audit.

fields.text: The Central Institute of Coir Technology is at
fields.explanation: The Central Institute of Coir Technology is located in Bangalore.

fields.text: The year in which the Kerala State Co-operative Societies Act was passed in Kerala
fields.explanation: The Kerala State Co-operative Societies Act was passed in 1969.

fields.text: The Kerala Co-operative Societies (Amendment) Act was passed in:
fields.explanation: The Amendment Act was passed in 2013.

fields.text: Who published the publication "Co-operator" in England?
fields.explanation: William King published the "Co-operator" to promote cooperative ideas.

fields.text: Who can convene the last general body of a society?
fields.explanation: The Liquidator convenes the last general body meeting during winding-up.

fields.text: The term "Co-operation" is derived from the word 'Co-operari' which means
fields.explanation: "Co-operation" comes from the Latin 'Co-operari,' meaning to work together.

fields.text: Equitable distribution of surplus is one of the pivotal principles followed by
fields.explanation: Equitable surplus distribution is a cooperative principle, not typical of HUF, companies, or partnerships.

fields.text: Only a person who has attained the age of years can become a member in a co-operative society.

options.A: twenty one for full membership and fifteen for minors
options.B: twenty one for full membership and eighteen for beneficial members

fields.explanation: A person must be at least eighteen to join a cooperative society.

fields.text: The birth of Co-operative movement is in
fields.explanation: The cooperative movement began in England with the Rochdale Society in 1844.

fields.text: The Co-operative Farming Societies come under the category of
fields.explanation: Co-operative Farming Societies are non-credit agricultural cooperatives.

fields.text: Gaya diary brand's owner is
fields.explanation: The Gaya dairy brand is owned by the Bihar State Milk Producers Union.

fields.text: Who was the first Co-operative Minister of Kerala?
fields.explanation: Baby John was the first Co-operative Minister of Kerala.
