fields.text: Mention the name of Kerala Handicraft Apex Co-operative Societies.
fields.explanation: Surabhi is Kerala's Handicraft Apex Co-operative Society.

fields.text: The year in which Matsya fed is registered as a Co-operative institution:
fields.explanation: Matsya Fed was registered as a cooperative in 1984.

fields.text: Who started urban banks in Italy?
fields.explanation: Luigi Luzzatti (Lusy Lussati) started urban banks in Italy.

fields.text: KRIBHCO is an
fields.explanation: KRIBHCO is an interstate cooperative institution in India.

fields.text: D. R. Gadgil is well known as the
fields.explanation: D. R. Gadgil is known as the Father of co-operative commonwealth in India.

fields.text: Who took over the activities of Agricultural Refinance & Development Corporation stopped its functions in 1982?
fields.explanation: NABARD took over ARDC's functions in 1982.

fields.text: Headquarters of NAFED is situated at:
fields.explanation: NAFED's headquarters is in New Delhi.

fields.text: Section of the Kerala Co-operative Societies Act gives provisions relating to winding up of bank coming under DICGC.
fields.explanation: Section 74A provides for winding up banks under DICGC.

fields.text: When a member can exercise his right to withdraw his share from a cooperative society?
fields.explanation: Shares in a cooperative society are typically non-withdrawable.

fields.text: National Centre for Co-operative Education is located at:
fields.explanation: The National Centre for Co-operative Education is in New Delhi.

fields.text: Which Committee recommended for the formation of Farmers Service Co-operative Society?
fields.explanation: The T.A. Pai Committee recommended Farmers Service Co-operative Societies.

fields.text: Narasimham Committee (1975) recommended for the formation of:
fields.explanation: The Narasimham Committee recommended Regional Rural Banks in 1975.

fields.text: The year in which the National Policy on Co-operation is declared in India:
fields.explanation: The National Policy on Co-operation was declared in 2000.

fields.text: Expand CRAFICARD:

options.A: Committee to Review Arrangements for Institutional Credit for Agricultural and Rural Development
options.B: Committee to Review Aid for Institutional Credit for Agricultural and Rural Development
options.C: Committee to Review Arrangements for Institutional Credit for Agricultural and Regional Development

fields.explanation: CRAFICARD is the Committee to Review Arrangements for Institutional Credit for Agricultural and Rural Development.

fields.text: The area of activities of a Circle Co-operative Society is limited:
fields.explanation: Circle Co-operative Societies operate at the taluk level.

fields.text: Vaikunt Mehta National Institute of Co-operative Management is located at:
fields.explanation: The Vaikunt Mehta National Institute is located in Pune.

fields.text: In a Co-operative society Revenue Fund is created out of
fields.explanation: The Revenue Fund is created from the net profit of a cooperative society.

fields.text: Sputnik is the first Co-operative Societies Movement in
fields.explanation: Sputnik was the first cooperative movement in Russia.

fields.text: The process of thoroughly checking the collected data to ensure optimal quality level is referred to as:
fields.explanation: Editing involves checking data to ensure its quality and accuracy.

fields.text: When a person transacts with a company on matters which is beyond the powers of the company, the person will be governed by the Doctrine of
fields.explanation: The Doctrine of Constructive Notice applies to transactions beyond a company's powers.

fields.text: The Rochdale Society of Equitable Pioneers was established in which year?
fields.explanation: The Rochdale Society was founded in 1844 in England and is considered the foundation of modern cooperative movement.

fields.text: How many principles were originally formulated by the Rochdale Pioneers?
fields.explanation: The Rochdale Pioneers originally formulated 8 principles, which later became the foundation for modern cooperative principles.

fields.text: According to ICA, how many cooperative principles are there currently?
fields.explanation: The International Cooperative Alliance (ICA) currently recognizes 7 cooperative principles.

fields.text: Which is the first cooperative principle according to ICA?
fields.explanation: Voluntary and Open Membership is the first principle, emphasizing that cooperatives are open to all without discrimination.

fields.text: The principle "One Member, One Vote" is related to which cooperative principle?
fields.explanation: Democratic Member Control ensures equal voting rights regardless of the amount of capital contributed.

fields.text: Which cooperative principle emphasizes that members contribute equitably to capital?
fields.explanation: Member Economic Participation involves members contributing equitably to and democratically controlling capital.

fields.text: The Father of Cooperation is considered to be:
fields.explanation: Robert Owen is widely regarded as the Father of Cooperation for his pioneering work in cooperative principles.

fields.text: MILMA stands for:
fields.explanation: MILMA is the popular name for Kerala Co-operative Milk Marketing Federation established in 1980.

fields.text: The Kerala State Co-operative Societies Act was passed in:
fields.explanation: The Kerala State Co-operative Societies Act was enacted in 1969.

fields.text: The minimum number of members required to form a primary cooperative society is:
fields.explanation: According to Kerala Cooperative Societies Act, minimum 25 members from different families are required.

fields.text: The term "cooperation" is derived from the Latin word:
fields.explanation: "Cooperation" comes from the Latin word "Cooperari" meaning "to work together".

fields.text: Which cooperative principle deals with providing education to members?
fields.explanation: The Fifth Principle is "Education, Training and Information" which emphasizes member education.

fields.text: The International Co-operative Alliance (ICA) was formed in:
fields.explanation: ICA was established in 1895 as the first international association of the cooperative movement.

fields.text: What is the maximum voting right of a member in a cooperative society?
fields.explanation: Cooperative principle of democratic control ensures "one member, one vote" regardless of shareholding.

fields.text: NAFED was established in:
fields.explanation: National Agricultural Cooperative Marketing Federation was established on 2nd October 1958.

fields.text: The cooperative principle that emphasizes working together with other cooperatives is:
fields.explanation: The Sixth Principle is "Cooperation among Cooperatives" which promotes inter-cooperative collaboration.

fields.text: In a cooperative society, the liability of members is:
fields.explanation: Members of cooperative societies have limited liability, restricted to their share capital contribution.

fields.text: The headquarters of ICA is located in:
fields.explanation: The International Cooperative Alliance headquarters is in Brussels, Belgium.

fields.text: Which committee recommended the formation of NABARD?
fields.explanation: The Sivaraman Committee recommended the establishment of NABARD in 1979.

fields.text: The cooperative emblem contains how many colors?
fields.explanation: The cooperative rainbow flag contains 7 colors representing the 7 cooperative principles.

fields.text: What percentage of net profit should be transferred to reserve fund in a cooperative society?
fields.explanation: At least 15% of net profits must be transferred to the reserve fund annually.

fields.text: The first cooperative credit society in India was established at:
fields.explanation: The first cooperative credit society was established at Kanaginahal in Karnataka in 1904.

fields.text: KRIBHCO stands for:
fields.explanation: KRIBHCO is Krishak Bharati Cooperative Limited, a fertilizer cooperative.

fields.text: Who is known as the Father of Rural Credit in India?
fields.explanation: Frederick Nicholson is called the Father of Rural Credit for his work on cooperative credit in India.

fields.text: The Indian Cooperative Credit Societies Act was passed in:
fields.explanation: The first cooperative legislation in India was the Cooperative Credit Societies Act of 1904.

fields.text: Amul is located in which state?
fields.explanation: Amul (Anand Milk Union Limited) is located in Anand, Gujarat.

fields.text: The seventh cooperative principle is:
fields.explanation: "Concern for Community" is the seventh principle, emphasizing sustainable community development.

fields.text: In Kerala, who is the Registrar of Cooperative Societies?
fields.explanation: The Director of Cooperation serves as the Registrar of Cooperative Societies in Kerala.

fields.text: The cooperative principle of "Autonomy and Independence" is the:
fields.explanation: "Autonomy and Independence" is the Fourth Principle, ensuring cooperatives remain autonomous organizations.

fields.text: The Constitutional Amendment that lowered the voting age from 21 to 18 years:
fields.explanation: The 61st Constitutional Amendment Act of 1988 reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

fields.text: The President of India is elected by:
fields.explanation: The President is elected through an Electoral College consisting of elected members of both Houses of Parliament and Legislative Assemblies of states.

fields.text: The Quit India Movement was launched in:
fields.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi on August 8, 1942, demanding immediate independence from British rule.

fields.text: The first session of Indian National Congress was held at:
fields.explanation: The first session of the Indian National Congress was held in Bombay (now Mumbai) in December 1885 under A.O. Hume.

fields.text: The Sepoy Mutiny of 1857 started from:
fields.explanation: The Sepoy Mutiny of 1857 began in Meerut on May 10, 1857, and later spread to other parts of northern India.

fields.text: The Tropic of Cancer passes through which Indian state:
fields.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer passes through Gujarat, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Tripura, and Mizoram.

fields.text: The highest peak in Western Ghats is:
fields.explanation: Anai Mudi (2,695 meters) in Kerala is the highest peak in the Western Ghats and South India.

fields.text: The longest river in India is:
fields.explanation: The Ganga is the longest river in India, flowing for about 2,525 kilometers from Gangotri to the Bay of Bengal.

fields.text: The process of conversion of sugar into alcohol is called:
fields.explanation: Fermentation is the process where microorganisms convert sugar into alcohol and carbon dioxide.

fields.text: The hardest natural substance found on Earth is:
fields.explanation: Diamond is the hardest natural substance on Earth, rating 10 on the Mohs hardness scale.

fields.text: The study of earthquakes is called:
fields.explanation: Seismology is the scientific study of earthquakes and the propagation of elastic waves through the Earth.

fields.text: The first Malayalam newspaper was:
fields.explanation: Rajyasamacharam, published in 1847 by Hermann Gundert, was the first Malayalam newspaper.

fields.text: The largest district in Kerala by area is:
fields.explanation: Idukki is the largest district in Kerala by area, covering 4,358 square kilometers.

fields.text: Kerala's first hydro-electric project was:
fields.explanation: Pallivasal, commissioned in 1940, was Kerala's first hydro-electric project, located in Idukki district.

fields.text: The term 'Repo Rate' refers to:

options.A: Rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks
options.B: Rate at which banks lend to customers

fields.explanation: Repo Rate is the rate at which the Reserve Bank of India lends money to commercial banks for short-term needs.

fields.text: GST was implemented in India in:
fields.explanation: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) was implemented in India on July 1, 2017.

fields.text: The monetary policy in India is formulated by:
fields.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) formulates and implements monetary policy in India.

fields.text: What is the headquarters of KSEB known as?
fields.explanation: The headquarters of the Kerala State Electricity Board (KSEB) is known as "Vaidyuti Bhavanam" located in Thiruvananthapuram.

fields.text: Which place was historically known as Kovilkandi?
fields.explanation: Kovilkandi was the historical name for Koyilandy, a town in Kozhikode district.

fields.text: Which city was known as Ophir in ancient times?
fields.explanation: Ophir was the ancient name for Beypore, a port town in Kerala.

fields.text: Who was the first president of the Malabar District Board?
fields.explanation: P.T. Bhaskara Panicker was the first president of the Malabar District Board.

fields.text: Who is known as the storyteller of Athiranippadam?
fields.explanation: S.K. Pottekkatt is known as the storyteller of Athiranippadam, referring to his literary works set in Malabar.

fields.text: Whose autobiography is 'Arangukaanatha Nadan'?
fields.explanation: 'Arangukaanatha Nadan' is the autobiography of Thikkodiyan, a Malayalam playwright.

fields.text: Which was the first complete blood donation panchayat in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Madikkai in Kasaragod district was the first panchayat in Kerala to achieve 100% blood donation.

fields.text: Which is the first district in India without landless people?
fields.explanation: Kannur was declared the first landless-free district in India under a government initiative.

fields.text: Where is the first mangrove museum in Kerala located?
fields.explanation: The first mangrove museum in Kerala is located in Koyilandy.

fields.text: Where is the first gender park in Malabar located?
fields.explanation: The first gender park in Malabar is located in Vellimadukunnu, Kozhikode.

fields.text: Who founded the Cochin State?
fields.explanation: The Cochin State was founded by the Perumpadappu Swaroopam dynasty.

fields.text: Which Kerala ruler is known as the maker of modern Travancore?
fields.explanation: Marthanda Varma is known as the maker of modern Travancore for his administrative and military reforms.

fields.text: The Guruvayur Satyagraha was led by:
fields.explanation: K. Kelappan led the Guruvayur Satyagraha for temple entry rights.

fields.text: The Vaikom Satyagraha was held in which year?
fields.explanation: The Vaikom Satyagraha was held from 1924-25 for temple entry rights.

fields.text: Which dance form is known as the classical dance of Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kathakali is recognized as the classical dance-drama form of Kerala.

fields.text: The Kochi-Muziris Biennale is held every:
fields.explanation: The Kochi-Muziris Biennale is held every two years as an international art exhibition.

fields.text: Which is the oldest mosque in India?
fields.explanation: Cheraman Juma Mosque in Thrissur is considered the oldest mosque in India.

fields.text: The Sree Narayana Guru was born in:
fields.explanation: Sree Narayana Guru was born in Chempazhanthi, Thiruvananthapuram.

fields.text: Which European power first established trade relations with Kerala?
fields.explanation: The Portuguese were the first Europeans to establish trade relations with Kerala in 1498.

fields.text: The Aikya Kerala movement was launched in:
fields.explanation: The Aikya Kerala movement was launched in 1956 to unite Malayalam-speaking regions.

fields.text: Which river carries the most water in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Periyar is the longest and largest river in Kerala in terms of water volume.

fields.text: Iruvanjippuzha is a major tributary of which river?
fields.explanation: Iruvanjippuzha is a major tributary of the Chaliyar River in Kerala.

fields.text: Where was the world's oldest teak tree found?
fields.explanation: The oldest teak tree, known as "Kannimara Teak," is located in Nilambur, Kerala.

fields.text: Which is the highest peak in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Anamudi in Idukki district is the highest peak in Kerala at 2,695 meters.

fields.text: Which is the largest lake in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Vembanad Lake is the largest lake in Kerala and the longest lake in India.

fields.text: Which district has the longest coastline in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kannur district has the longest coastline in Kerala.

fields.text: The Western Ghats in Kerala are also known as:
fields.explanation: The Western Ghats are also known as Sahyadri mountains.

fields.text: Which is the smallest district in Kerala by area?
fields.explanation: Alappuzha is the smallest district in Kerala by area.

fields.text: The Agasthyamalai Biosphere Reserve is located in:
fields.explanation: Agasthyamalai Biosphere Reserve is primarily located in Thiruvananthapuram district.

fields.text: Which river is known as the Ganga of Kerala?
fields.explanation: Bharathapuzha is known as the Ganga of Kerala due to its cultural significance.

fields.text: The Cardamom Hills are located in:
fields.explanation: The Cardamom Hills are primarily located in Idukki district.

fields.text: Which is the largest wildlife sanctuary in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary is the largest wildlife sanctuary in Kerala.

fields.text: The Palghat Gap connects Kerala with:
fields.explanation: The Palghat Gap is a mountain pass that connects Kerala with Tamil Nadu.

fields.text: Which district is known as the Rice Bowl of Kerala?
fields.explanation: Palakkad district is known as the Rice Bowl of Kerala due to its extensive paddy cultivation.

fields.text: The Munnar hill station is located in:
fields.explanation: Munnar hill station is located in Idukki district.

fields.text: Which is the only district in Kerala without a sea coast?
fields.explanation: Both Idukki and Wayanad districts do not have a sea coast.

fields.text: The Kuttanad region is famous for:
fields.explanation: Kuttanad is famous for its below sea level farming, particularly rice cultivation.

fields.text: Which is the longest river in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Periyar is the longest river in Kerala at 244 km.

fields.text: The Backwaters of Kerala are primarily located in:
fields.explanation: The backwaters of Kerala are distributed across Alappuzha, Kottayam, and Kollam districts.

fields.text: Which country provided assistance for the Urumi hydroelectric project?
fields.explanation: China provided assistance for the Urumi hydroelectric project in Kerala.

fields.text: What happens to the pupil of the eye in bright light?
fields.explanation: In bright light, the pupil of the eye constricts to reduce the amount of light entering.

fields.text: Which metal alloy is used for making aircraft parts?
fields.explanation: Duralumin, an aluminum alloy, is used in aircraft manufacturing due to its strength and lightweight properties.

fields.text: The scientific study of snakes is called:
fields.explanation: Ophiology is the branch of science that studies snakes.

fields.text: Which part of the brain processes pain signals?
fields.explanation: The thalamus in the brain is responsible for processing pain signals, and analgesics target this area.

fields.text: A piece of land surrounded by water on three sides is called:
fields.explanation: A piece of land surrounded by water on three sides is called a peninsula.

fields.text: The process of plants making their own food using sunlight is called:
fields.explanation: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants make their own food using sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide.

fields.text: Which vitamin is produced when skin is exposed to sunlight?
fields.explanation: Vitamin D is produced in the skin when exposed to sunlight.

fields.text: The hardest natural substance on Earth is:
fields.explanation: Diamond is the hardest natural substance on Earth.

fields.text: Which gas is most abundant in Earth's atmosphere?
fields.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of Earth's atmosphere.

fields.text: Which organ in the human body produces insulin?
fields.explanation: The pancreas produces insulin, which regulates blood sugar levels.

fields.text: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately:
fields.explanation: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately 3 × 10^8 meters per second.

fields.text: Which blood group is known as the universal donor?
fields.explanation: O negative blood group is known as the universal donor.

fields.text: The chemical symbol for gold is:
fields.explanation: Au is the chemical symbol for gold, derived from the Latin word "aurum".

fields.text: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
fields.explanation: Mars is known as the Red Planet due to its reddish appearance.

fields.text: The unit of electric current is:
fields.explanation: Ampere is the unit of electric current.

fields.text: Which scientist proposed the theory of evolution?
fields.explanation: Charles Darwin proposed the theory of evolution by natural selection.

fields.text: The largest organ in the human body is:
fields.explanation: The skin is the largest organ in the human body.

fields.text: Which gas is commonly known as laughing gas?
fields.explanation: Nitrous oxide (N2O) is commonly known as laughing gas.

fields.text: The process by which water changes from liquid to gas is called:
fields.explanation: Evaporation is the process by which water changes from liquid to gas.

fields.text: In C language, a user-defined header is identified by:
fields.explanation: In C, user-defined header files are included using double quotes, while system-defined headers use angle brackets.

fields.text: Number of locations that could be addressed by a microprocessor with 12 address lines:
fields.explanation: A microprocessor with 12 address lines can address 2^12 = 4096 memory locations.

fields.text: The command that lists the last few lines from a file:
fields.explanation: The tail command in Unix/Linux displays the last few lines of a file.

fields.text: The percentage of time that a computer system is not available for use:
fields.explanation: Down time refers to the period when a system is unavailable for use.

fields.text: By threads of the same process, sharing is not allowed in:
fields.explanation: Each thread in a process has its own stack, which is not shared with other threads.

fields.text: How to declare an interface class in C++?

options.A: By declaring all methods pure virtual in a class
options.B: Make all methods abstract in a class

fields.explanation: In C++, an interface is created by declaring a class with all methods as pure virtual (using = 0).

fields.text: Which device translates between data formats?
fields.explanation: Gateways translate between different data formats or protocols in a network.

fields.text: If a relation is in BCNF then it is in:
fields.explanation: A relation in Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF) is also in 3NF, as BCNF is a stricter form of normalization.

fields.text: The OSI model has how many layers?
fields.explanation: The OSI (Open Systems Interconnection) model has 7 layers.

fields.text: Which of the following is not an operating system?
fields.explanation: Oracle is a database management system, not an operating system.

fields.text: What does CPU stand for?
fields.explanation: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit.

fields.text: Which programming language is known as the mother of all languages?
fields.explanation: C is often called the mother of all programming languages due to its influence.

fields.text: What is the full form of HTML?
fields.explanation: HTML stands for Hyper Text Markup Language.

fields.text: Which of the following is a database management system?
fields.explanation: MySQL, Oracle, and SQL Server are all database management systems.

fields.text: What does WWW stand for?
fields.explanation: WWW stands for World Wide Web.

fields.text: In C language an user defined header is identified by the inclusion:
fields.explanation: In C, user-defined header files are included using double quotes (e.g., #include "test.h"), while system-defined headers use angle brackets (e.g., #include <stdio.h>).

fields.text: By default any real number in C is represented as:
fields.explanation: In C, real numbers are represented as double by default.

fields.text: Integer division in C program results in:
fields.explanation: Integer division in C truncates the decimal part, discarding the remainder.

fields.text: The function sprintf() works like printf(), but operates on:
fields.explanation: sprintf() formats data into a string, unlike printf(), which outputs to the console.

fields.text: The function exit() causes an exit from:
fields.explanation: The exit() function terminates the entire program.

fields.text: In C language data type of "3" is:
fields.explanation: In C, "3" (in quotes) is a string literal, but as a single character, it's treated as a char.

fields.text: What was the first Scientific Computer Programming Language?
fields.explanation: FORTRAN was the first high-level scientific programming language, developed in the 1950s.

fields.text: Which is the first Object Oriented Language?
fields.explanation: ADA is considered the first object-oriented programming language, designed in the late 1970s.

fields.text: The property that OOPs will permit the same operation to be carried out differently, based on the object is:
fields.explanation: Polymorphism allows different objects to respond to the same operation in different ways.

fields.text: Which of the following is used for modular programming in C?
fields.explanation: Functions enable modular programming by breaking code into reusable modules.

fields.text: The keyword used to prevent any changes in the variable within a C program is:
fields.explanation: The const keyword makes a variable read-only.

fields.text: Which operator is used for dynamic memory allocation in C++?
fields.explanation: The new operator is used for dynamic memory allocation in C++.

fields.text: What is the size of int data type in C (on 32-bit system)?
fields.explanation: On a 32-bit system, int typically occupies 4 bytes.

fields.text: Which function is used to find the length of a string in C?
fields.explanation: strlen() function returns the length of a string.

fields.text: What does the preprocessor directive #define do?
fields.explanation: #define creates macros for text replacement.

fields.text: Which header file is required for input/output operations in C?
fields.explanation: stdio.h contains declarations for standard input/output functions.

fields.text: What is the correct way to declare a pointer in C?
fields.explanation: The asterisk (

fields.text: Which loop is guaranteed to execute at least once?
fields.explanation: do-while loop executes the body at least once before checking the condition.

fields.text: What is the purpose of break statement in C?

options.A: To exit from a loop or switch

fields.explanation: break statement is used to exit from loops or switch statements.

fields.text: Number of locations that could be addressed by a microprocessor with 12 address lines is:
fields.explanation: A microprocessor with 12 address lines can address (2^12 = 4096) memory locations.

fields.text: Number of bit width of any memory chip is:
fields.explanation: The bit width of a standard memory chip is typically 8 bits, corresponding to a byte.

fields.text: Which is 16 bit microprocessor?
fields.explanation: The Intel 8086 is a 16-bit microprocessor.

fields.text: Register used as working area in CPU is:
fields.explanation: The accumulator is the primary register for arithmetic and logical operations in the CPU.

fields.text: A microprocessor can be:
fields.explanation: Microprocessors are programmable devices that execute instructions.

fields.text: The Virtual Memory is located logically:

options.C: in between CPU and primary memory

fields.explanation: Virtual memory is logically located on the hard disk, used to extend RAM capacity.

fields.text: Why RAM is kept near to the Processor in a Digital Computer?
fields.explanation: RAM is placed near the processor to minimize memory access time, improving performance.

fields.text: The cycle time for a memory system is:
fields.explanation: Cycle time is the total time for a memory operation, typically longer than access time.

fields.text: Which is non-volatile?
fields.explanation: ROM (Read-Only Memory) is non-volatile, retaining data without power.

fields.text: Which is the access method to take data from tape memory?
fields.explanation: Tape memory uses sequential access, reading data in the order it is stored.

fields.text: What is the working Principle of a Digital Computer?
fields.explanation: Digital computers operate on the Von Neumann architecture, with stored programs and data.

fields.text: Give the unique name of the combination of Analog and Digital Computer:
fields.explanation: A hybrid computer combines analog and digital computing capabilities.

fields.text: The switching devices used in First Generation computers were:
fields.explanation: First-generation computers (1940s-1950s) used vacuum tubes as switching devices.

fields.text: Name the component carrying the ALU and Control Unit in Digital Computer:
fields.explanation: The microprocessor contains the ALU and Control Unit.

fields.text: What is a BUFFER?

options.C: An Interface between CPU and peripheral

fields.explanation: A buffer acts as an interface to manage data transfer between the CPU and peripherals.

fields.text: Resolution of Video display unit does not depend on:
fields.explanation: Screen resolution depends on raster scan rate, bandwidth, and scan lines, not the physical screen size.

fields.text: How many RAM chips are needed to generate the (256K X 1 bit)?
fields.explanation: A 256K x 1-bit memory requires 64 chips, assuming each chip provides 4K x 1-bit storage.

fields.text: Which of the following interrupt is both level and edge sensitive?
fields.explanation: RST 7.5 in the 8085 microprocessor is both level and edge sensitive.

fields.text: Which is a programmable interval timer?
fields.explanation: The Intel 8253 is a programmable interval timer.

fields.text: Flip-flop is the basic structure of the device:
fields.explanation: Flip-flops are fundamental components of memory circuits, such as registers.

fields.text: Which is fastest?
fields.explanation: ECL (Emitter-Coupled Logic) is the fastest among the given logic families due to its high-speed switching.

fields.text: Which is commutative but not associative?
fields.explanation: XOR is commutative (A XOR B = B XOR A) but not associative (A XOR (B XOR C) ≠ (A XOR B) XOR C).

fields.text: Which is Universal gate?
fields.explanation: NOR is a universal gate, as it can be used to implement any logical function.

fields.text: How many NAND gates are needed to perform X . Y?
fields.explanation: To implement X AND Y using NAND gates, 4 NAND gates are required (one for AND, others for inversion).

fields.text: T flip-flops are generally used for:
fields.explanation: T flip-flops are used in counters and frequency dividers due to their toggling behavior.

fields.text: "n" flip-flops will divide the frequency into a factor of:
fields.explanation: n flip-flops in a ripple counter divide the frequency by 2^n.

fields.text: 8 2 4 1 is the:
fields.explanation: The 8-4-2-1 code is the Binary-Coded Decimal (BCD) representation.

fields.text: The 2's complement of the binary value 10 is:
fields.explanation: The 2's complement of binary 10 (decimal 2) in a 2-bit system is 01.

fields.text: Gray code for the decimal number 7 is:
fields.explanation: The Gray code for decimal 7 (binary 111) is 0101, derived by XORing adjacent bits.

fields.text: (1001000010011001) base 2; equivalent in base 16 is:
fields.explanation: Binary (1001000010011001) converts to hexadecimal (8999).

fields.text: Negative number cannot be represented in:
fields.explanation: All listed forms can represent negative numbers.

fields.text: Which of the following is a combinational circuit?
fields.explanation: A decoder is a combinational circuit as its output depends only on current inputs.

fields.text: How many input combinations are possible with 4 input lines?
fields.explanation: With 4 input lines, there are 2^4 = 16 possible input combinations.

fields.text: Which gate produces HIGH output only when all inputs are HIGH?
fields.explanation: AND gate produces HIGH output only when all inputs are HIGH.

fields.text: The number of rows in a truth table with n variables is:
fields.explanation: A truth table with n variables has 2^n rows.

fields.text: Which of the following is used to store one bit of data?
fields.explanation: A flip-flop is a sequential circuit that can store one bit of data.

fields.text: The Boolean expression A + A'B is equivalent to:
fields.explanation: Using Boolean algebra, A + A'B = A + B (absorption theorem).

fields.text: How many AND gates are required to realize Y = ABCD?
fields.explanation: To realize 4-input AND gate using 2-input AND gates, 3 gates are needed.

fields.text: Which number system uses base 8?
fields.explanation: Octal number system uses base 8.

fields.text: The complement of (A + B) is:
fields.explanation: By De Morgan's law, (A + B)' = A'.B'.

fields.text: The percentage of time that a computer system is not available for use is:
fields.explanation: Down time refers to the period when a computer system is unavailable for use.

fields.text: By the thread of the same process, sharing is not allowed in:
fields.explanation: Each thread in a process has its own stack, which is not shared with other threads.

fields.text: The Banker's algorithm in resource allocation deals with:
fields.explanation: The Banker's algorithm is used to avoid deadlocks by ensuring safe resource allocation.

fields.text: Thrashing occurs in:

options.B: a natural consequence of the virtual memory system
options.C: can always be avoided by swapping

fields.explanation: Thrashing is a natural consequence of virtual memory systems when excessive paging occurs due to insufficient physical memory.

fields.text: Which is not possible in a thread?
fields.explanation: Threads can be in Ready, Suspended, or Running states, but "Wait" is not a standard thread state.

fields.text: Producer consumer problem is example for application of:

options.B: For Monitors, Semaphores and Event counters

fields.explanation: The producer-consumer problem is typically solved using monitors for synchronization.

fields.text: Give the name of the first Operating system for Personal Computers:
fields.explanation: CP/M was the first widely used operating system for personal computers in the 1970s.

fields.text: Which among the following is not a reason for process termination?
fields.explanation: Invalid time is not a standard reason for process termination.

fields.text: Which among the following will not lead to a deadlock?
fields.explanation: Preemption prevents deadlocks by allowing resource reallocation.

fields.text: Which level of security is implemented in Windows NT?
fields.explanation: Windows NT implements C2-level security.

fields.text: The command that lists the last few lines from a file named "file":
fields.explanation: The tail command in Unix/Linux displays the last few lines of a file.

fields.text: What is multitasking in operating systems?

options.B: Running one program at a time

fields.explanation: Multitasking allows multiple programs to run concurrently.

fields.text: Which of the following is a real-time operating system?

fields.text: How many Municipalities are there in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kerala has 87 Municipalities for urban local governance.

fields.text: How many Assembly constituencies are there in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kerala Legislative Assembly has 140 constituencies.

fields.text: How many Assembly seats are there in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kerala Legislative Assembly has 141 seats including the Speaker.

fields.text: How many Scheduled Caste reserved constituencies are there in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kerala has 14 Assembly constituencies reserved for Scheduled Castes.

fields.text: How many Scheduled Tribe reserved constituencies are there in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kerala has 2 Assembly constituencies reserved for Scheduled Tribes (Sultan Bathery and Mananthavady).

fields.text: How many Lok Sabha constituencies are there in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kerala is represented by 20 constituencies in the Lok Sabha.

fields.text: How many reserved Lok Sabha constituencies are there in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kerala has 2 Lok Sabha constituencies reserved for Scheduled Castes (Alathur and Mavelikkara).

fields.text: How many Rajya Sabha seats does Kerala have?
fields.explanation: Kerala is allocated 9 seats in the Rajya Sabha.

fields.text: What is the length of Kerala's coastline?
fields.explanation: Kerala has a coastline of 580 kilometers along the Arabian Sea.

fields.text: How many districts in Kerala have coastal areas?
fields.explanation: 9 out of 14 districts in Kerala have coastal areas.

fields.text: How many rivers are there in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kerala has a total of 44 rivers.

fields.text: How many rivers flow westward in Kerala?
fields.explanation: 41 rivers in Kerala flow westward into the Arabian Sea.

fields.text: How many rivers flow eastward in Kerala?
fields.explanation: 3 rivers flow eastward in Kerala (Kabani, Bhavani, and Pambar).

fields.text: How many backwaters are there in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kerala has 34 backwaters (kayals) that form part of its unique ecosystem.

fields.text: What is the life expectancy in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kerala has a life expectancy of 73.8 years, which is higher than the national average.

fields.text: Which district has the highest Scheduled Caste population in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Palakkad district has the highest Scheduled Caste population in Kerala.

fields.text: Which district has the lowest Scheduled Caste population in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Wayanad district has the lowest Scheduled Caste population in Kerala.

fields.text: Which district has the highest Scheduled Tribe population in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Wayanad district has the highest Scheduled Tribe population in Kerala.

fields.text: Which district has the lowest Scheduled Tribe population in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Alappuzha district has the lowest Scheduled Tribe population in Kerala.

fields.text: Which district has the highest per capita income in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Ernakulam district has the highest per capita income in Kerala.

fields.text: Which district has the lowest per capita income in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Alappuzha district has the lowest per capita income in Kerala.

fields.text: Which district has the largest forest area in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Idukki district has the largest forest area in Kerala.

fields.text: Which district has the smallest forest area in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Alappuzha district has the smallest forest area in Kerala.

fields.text: Which is the largest taluk in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Eranad is the largest taluk in Kerala by area.

fields.text: What is the official tree of Kerala?
fields.explanation: Coconut (Thenga in Malayalam) is the official tree of Kerala, reflecting its significance in the state's economy and culture.

fields.text: What is the official flower of Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kanikkonna (Golden Shower tree flower) is the official flower of Kerala, blooming during the Vishu season.

fields.text: What is the official drink of Kerala?
fields.explanation: Tender coconut water (Ilaneer in Malayalam) is the official drink of Kerala.

fields.text: Which village in Kerala leads in literacy?
fields.explanation: Nedumudi village in Alappuzha district holds the top position in literacy rate in Kerala.

fields.text: Which municipality in Kerala leads in literacy?
fields.explanation: Chengannur municipality leads in literacy rate among Kerala municipalities.

fields.text: Which was the first panchayat to achieve 100% literacy?
fields.explanation: Kariveellur panchayat in Kannur district was the first to achieve 100% literacy.

fields.text: Which is the largest municipality by area in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Thrippunithura municipality has the largest area among Kerala municipalities.

fields.text: Which is the smallest municipality by area in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Guruvayur is the smallest municipality by area in Kerala.

fields.text: Which taluk has the highest population in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kozhikode taluk has the highest population among all taluks in Kerala.

fields.text: Which taluk has the lowest population in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Mallappally taluk has the lowest population among all taluks in Kerala.

fields.text: Which corporation has the lowest population in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Thrissur corporation has the lowest population among Kerala corporations.

fields.text: Which corporation has the highest population in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Thiruvananthapuram corporation has the highest population among Kerala corporations.

fields.text: Which is the most industrialized district in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Ernakulam is the most industrialized district in Kerala, with major industrial centers like Kochi.

fields.text: Which is the second most industrialized district in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Palakkad is the second most industrialized district in Kerala.

fields.text: Which district has the highest Hindu population in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Thiruvananthapuram district has the highest Hindu population in Kerala.

fields.text: Which district has the highest Muslim population in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Malappuram district has the highest Muslim population in Kerala.

fields.text: Which district has the highest Christian population in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Ernakulam district has the highest Christian population in Kerala.

fields.text: Which district has the most post offices in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Thrissur district has the most post offices in Kerala.

fields.text: Which is the most recently formed district in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kasaragod is the most recently formed district in Kerala, created in 1984.

fields.text: Which is the largest gram panchayat by area in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kumily gram panchayat in Idukki district is the largest by area in Kerala.

fields.text: Which is the smallest gram panchayat by area in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Valapattanam gram panchayat in Kannur district is the smallest by area in Kerala.

fields.text: What is the only cantonment in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kannur is the only cantonment in Kerala.

fields.text: How many railway divisions are there in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Kerala has 2 railway divisions - Thiruvananthapuram and Palakkad.

fields.text: Which district has the most railway stations in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Thiruvananthapuram district has the most railway stations in Kerala.

fields.text: Who was the first Governor of Kerala?
fields.explanation: B. Ramakrishna Rao was the first Governor of Kerala after the state's formation in 1956.

fields.text: Who was the first woman Governor of Kerala?
fields.explanation: Jyoti Venkatachalam was the first woman Governor of Kerala.

fields.text: Who was the second woman Governor of Kerala?
fields.explanation: Ramdulari Singh was the second woman Governor of Kerala.

fields.text: Who was the third woman Governor of Kerala?
fields.explanation: Sheila Dikshit was the third woman Governor of Kerala.

fields.text: Which Kerala Governor died while in office?
fields.explanation: Sikander Bakht died while serving as Governor of Kerala.

fields.text: Who was the first Malayali woman Governor?
fields.explanation: Fathima Beevi was the first Malayali woman to serve as Governor (though not of Kerala).

fields.text: Who was the only Malayali to become Governor of Kerala?
fields.explanation: V. Vishwanathan was the only Malayali to serve as Governor of Kerala.

fields.text: Which Kerala Governor received the Bharat Ratna?
fields.explanation: V.V. Giri, who served as Kerala Governor, was awarded the Bharat Ratna.

fields.text: Which Malayali was Governor of Madras state in 1956?
fields.explanation: A.J. John was the Malayali who served as Governor of Madras state in 1956.

fields.text: In which year did elections occur but the Kerala Legislative Assembly was not formed?
fields.explanation: In 1965, elections were held but the Kerala Legislative Assembly was not formed due to political instability.

fields.text: Where was Electronic Voting Machine first used in India?
fields.explanation: Electronic Voting Machine was first used in India at North Paravur, Kerala in 1982.

fields.text: Who served as Kerala Governor for the longest period?
fields.explanation: V. Vishwanathan served as Kerala Governor for the longest period.

fields.text: When did Kerala state come into existence?
fields.explanation: Kerala state was formed on November 1, 1956, through the States Reorganization Act.

fields.text: How many districts were there when Kerala was formed in 1956?
fields.explanation: When Kerala was formed in 1956, it had only 5 districts.

fields.text: What is Kerala's rank among Indian states by area?
fields.explanation: Kerala ranks 22nd among Indian states by area.

fields.text: What is Kerala's rank among Indian states by population?
fields.explanation: Kerala ranks 13th among Indian states by population.

fields.text: Which beach is the background of the novel 'Chemmeen'?
fields.explanation: Purakkad beach in Alappuzha district is the setting for Thakazhi Sivasankara Pillai's famous novel 'Chemmeen', which won the Sahitya Akademi Award.

fields.text: What is the headquarters of Idukki district?
fields.explanation: Painav is the administrative headquarters of Idukki district, located in the Western Ghats region of Kerala.

fields.text: Where is the powerhouse of Idukki hydel project situated?
fields.explanation: Moolamattom is home to the powerhouse of the Idukki Hydel Project, one of the largest hydroelectric projects in Kerala.

fields.text: Which village in Idukki district was ceded to Ernakulam district, shifting Idukki to second rank in area?
fields.explanation: Kuttampuzha village was transferred from Idukki to Ernakulam district, making Idukki the second largest district in Kerala by area.

fields.text: Which island was formed after the flood of 1341?
fields.explanation: Vypin Island was formed after the great flood of 1341, which also changed the course of several rivers in Kerala.

fields.text: Where is INS Venduruthy situated?
fields.explanation: INS Venduruthy is the Indian Navy's communications training establishment located in Kochi, Ernakulam.

fields.text: In which district is Malayatoor, a holy place for Christians?
fields.explanation: Malayatoor is located in Ernakulam district and is believed to be the place where St. Thomas the Apostle prayed.

fields.text: Where is the Smart City Project implemented?
fields.explanation: Kochi was selected as one of the Smart Cities under the Government of India's Smart Cities Mission.

fields.text: Which temple is related to 'Makam Thozhal'?
fields.explanation: Chottanikkara Devi Temple is famous for the Makam Thozhal festival, celebrated during the Malayalam month of Makam.

fields.text: Which was the French occupied area in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Mahe was a French colony and is now a part of the Union Territory of Puducherry, located in Kannur district.

fields.text: In which district is Puralimala?
fields.explanation: Puralimala is a hill station located in Kannur district, known for its scenic beauty and trekking trails.

fields.text: In which district is Parassinikadav Muthappan temple?
fields.explanation: Parassinikadav Muthappan Temple is located in Kannur district, famous for the unique Theyyam ritual performances.

fields.text: Which place is known as 'Second Bardoli'?
fields.explanation: Payyannur in Kannur district is known as 'Second Bardoli' due to its role in the freedom struggle, similar to Bardoli in Gujarat.

fields.text: Which town is known as 'Circus town in Kerala'?
fields.explanation: Thalassery is known as the 'Circus town of Kerala' as it is the birthplace of circus in India, with many renowned circus artists hailing from here.

fields.text: Which fort in Kerala was built by Sivappa Naik of Bidanur?
fields.explanation: Bakel Fort was built by Sivappa Naik of Bidanur (Keladi dynasty) in the 17th century.

fields.text: Which pass connects Kerala and Kodagu?
fields.explanation: Perambady Pass connects Kerala with Kodagu (Coorg) district of Karnataka through the Western Ghats.

fields.text: Which place is known as 'the Cherrapunji of Kerala'?
fields.explanation: Lakkidi in Wayanad district receives very high rainfall and is called the 'Cherrapunji of Kerala'.

fields.text: Which European power built the Thalassery fort?
fields.explanation: Thalassery Fort was built by the British East India Company in 1708 for defense and trade purposes.

fields.text: Which district of Puducherry Union Territory shares border with Kerala?
fields.explanation: Mahe district of Puducherry Union Territory is located within Kerala's Kannur district and shares borders with Kerala.

fields.text: Which place was the centre of activities of Herman Gundert?
fields.explanation: Herman Gundert, the German missionary and Malayalam scholar, worked primarily in Thalassery where he compiled the first Malayalam-English dictionary.

fields.text: In which district is Mappila Bay harbour?
fields.explanation: Mappila Bay is a natural harbor located in Kannur district, historically significant for trade and commerce.

fields.text: What was the seat of Arakkal kingdom, the only Muslim dynasty in Kerala?
fields.explanation: The Arakkal kingdom, the only Muslim royal family in Kerala, had their seat at Kannur and ruled parts of northern Kerala.

fields.text: Which district in Kerala has the least number of Taluks?
fields.explanation: Wayanad district has only 3 taluks (Mananthavady, Sultanbathery, and Vythiri), the least among all Kerala districts.

fields.text: What is the northernmost backwater in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Uppala backwaters in Kasaragod district represent the northernmost backwater system in Kerala.

fields.text: Which area was called 'Martha' by foreigners?
fields.explanation: Karunagappally was known as 'Martha' by European traders and missionaries in historical times.

fields.text: In which district is Palaruvi waterfalls?
fields.explanation: Palaruvi waterfalls is located in Kollam district, known for its scenic beauty and medicinal properties of the water.

fields.text: In which district is the tourist spot Perumthenaruvi situated?
fields.explanation: Perumthenaruvi waterfalls is located in Pathanamthitta district, a popular tourist destination in the Western Ghats.

fields.text: In which district is Ramakkalmedu, the famous tourist centre?
fields.explanation: Ramakkalmedu is a hill station in Idukki district, famous for its wind farms and panoramic views of the Western Ghats.

fields.text: In which district is Veli tourist village?
fields.explanation: Veli tourist village is located in Thiruvananthapuram district, known for its backwater resort and floating bridge.

fields.text: Which place is famous for 'Muniyaras'?
fields.explanation: Marayur in Idukki district is famous for Muniyaras (dolmens), ancient megalithic burial chambers that are archaeological sites.

fields.text: Which place in Idukki district is famous for sandalwood forests?
fields.explanation: Marayur is famous for its natural sandalwood forests, one of the few places in Kerala where sandalwood grows naturally.

fields.text: Which place in Kerala is famous for 'Neelakurinji'?
fields.explanation: Munnar is famous for Neelakurinji flowers that bloom once every 12 years, covering the hills in blue color.

fields.text: Which place was known as 'Naura' in ancient period?
fields.explanation: Kannur was known as 'Naura' in ancient times, mentioned in various historical texts and maps.

fields.text: Where is the garden built in memory of national leaders?
fields.explanation: Peruvannamuzhi in Kozhikode district has a garden built in memory of national leaders and freedom fighters.

fields.text: Where is 'Joothakkulam' in Kerala?
fields.explanation: Joothakkulam is located in Madayi, Kannur district, known for its historical and archaeological significance.

fields.text: Where is Pazhassi Kudeeram situated?
fields.explanation: Pazhassi Kudeeram (Pazhassi's cave) is located in Mananthavady, Wayanad, associated with Kerala Varma Pazhassi Raja.

fields.text: Which area was once known as Chempakassery?
fields.explanation: Ambalappuzha was historically known as Chempakassery, an ancient principality in central Kerala.

fields.text: Which area was once known as Karappuram?
fields.explanation: Cherthala was historically known as Karappuram in ancient and medieval times.

fields.text: Which sanctuary was formerly known as Nellikkampatty?
fields.explanation: Thekkady Wildlife Sanctuary was formerly known as Nellikkampatty, now famous for the Periyar Tiger Reserve.

fields.text: Which river was known as 'Baris' in ancient times?
fields.explanation: The Pamba river was known as 'Baris' in ancient times, flowing through Pathanamthitta district to Sabarimala.

fields.text: Which region was known as 'Porainadu' in Sangam age?
fields.explanation: Palakkad region was known as 'Porainadu' during the Sangam period, mentioned in ancient Tamil literature.

fields.text: In which district are Edakkal Caves?
fields.explanation: Edakkal Caves are located in Wayanad district, famous for prehistoric petroglyphs and cave paintings.

fields.text: In which century did Kathakali originate?
fields.explanation: Kathakali, the classical dance-drama of Kerala, originated in the 17th century during the reign of various princely states.

fields.text: In which district is Agasthyarkoodam?
fields.explanation: Agasthyarkoodam (Agasthya Peak) is located in Thiruvananthapuram district, the second highest peak in Kerala.

fields.text: In which district are Athirappally-Vazhachal waterfalls?
fields.explanation: Athirappally and Vazhachal waterfalls are located in Thrissur district, popular tourist destinations known as the 'Niagara of India'.

fields.text: In which district is Kuruva Island?
fields.explanation: Kuruva Island is located in Wayanad district on the Kabini River, known for its rich flora and fauna.

fields.text: In which district is Sankhumukham beach?
fields.explanation: Sankhumukham beach is located in Thiruvananthapuram district, near the city center and famous for its sculpture park.

fields.text: In which district is Thattekkad Bird Sanctuary?
fields.explanation: Thattekkad Bird Sanctuary is located in Ernakulam district, also known as Salim Ali Bird Sanctuary.

fields.text: In which lake is Pathiramanal Island?
fields.explanation: Pathiramanal Island is located in Vembanad Lake, accessible from Kumarakom and famous for bird watching.

fields.text: In which place is Papanasam beach?
fields.explanation: Papanasam beach is located in Varkala, Thiruvananthapuram district, famous for its cliffs and believed to have holy waters.

fields.text: When did the Cyber Law come into effect in India?
fields.explanation: The Information Technology Act (Cyber Law) came into effect in India on October 17, 2000, making India one of the early adopters of cyber legislation.

fields.text: When providing oral hygiene to an unconscious patient, care must be taken to prevent?
fields.explanation: Aspiration is the most serious risk when providing oral hygiene to unconscious patients as they cannot control their swallowing reflex, potentially leading to airway obstruction or pneumonia.

fields.text: Which of the following structures is the pacemaker of the heart?
fields.explanation: The sinoatrial (S-A) node is the natural pacemaker of the heart, initiating electrical impulses that regulate heart rhythm.

fields.text: Which of the following is intravascular fluid?
fields.explanation: Plasma is the liquid component of blood that circulates within blood vessels (intravascular), while other options are extravascular fluids.

fields.text: If the serum potassium level is less than normal, the condition is known as?
fields.explanation: Hypokalemia refers to low potassium levels in blood serum (below 3.5 mEq/L), which can cause muscle weakness and cardiac arrhythmias.

fields.text: What is the primary cause of hyperkalemia?
fields.explanation: Renal failure is the primary cause of hyperkalemia as the kidneys are unable to effectively excrete excess potassium from the body.

fields.text: Which of the following is an isotonic solution?
fields.explanation: Lactated Ringer's solution is isotonic, meaning it has the same osmotic pressure as body fluids, making it ideal for fluid replacement.

fields.text: Which of the following is a first-line drug used for pain management?
fields.explanation: Acetaminophen (paracetamol) is a first-line analgesic for mild to moderate pain due to its safety profile and effectiveness.

fields.text: Which of the following is the organism that causes peptic ulcer disease?
fields.explanation: Helicobacter pylori is the primary bacterial cause of peptic ulcer disease, discovered by Barry Marshall and Robin Warren.

fields.text: Which of the following nutrients helps for tissue repair?
fields.explanation: Proteins are essential for tissue repair and wound healing as they provide amino acids needed for cellular regeneration.

fields.text: Which of the following patient activities helps to relax the external anal sphincter while administering enema?
fields.explanation: Breathing out through the mouth helps relax the external anal sphincter, making enema administration more comfortable and effective.

fields.text: Which of the following is the best method to confirm placement of the nasogastric tube?
fields.explanation: X-ray of chest and abdomen is the gold standard for confirming nasogastric tube placement, providing visual confirmation of tube position.

fields.text: Which of the following body parts has a condyloid joint?
fields.explanation: The wrist has condyloid joints that allow movement in two planes - flexion/extension and abduction/adduction.

fields.text: Which of the following minerals should be taken by a child suffering from rickets?
fields.explanation: Calcium is essential for bone development and mineralization. Rickets is caused by calcium and vitamin D deficiency.

fields.text: Which of the following drugs will be administered to treat anaphylaxis?
fields.explanation: Epinephrine (adrenaline) is the first-line treatment for anaphylaxis, rapidly reversing life-threatening symptoms.

fields.text: Which of the following immunoglobulin are responsible for anaphylactic reactions?
fields.explanation: IgE antibodies mediate immediate hypersensitivity reactions including anaphylaxis by triggering mast cell degranulation.

fields.text: Which of the following is an earliest manifestation of inadequate oxygenation?
fields.explanation: Restlessness is often the earliest sign of hypoxia as the brain responds to decreased oxygen levels before other symptoms appear.

fields.text: Which of the following is a normal finding obtained during lymph node palpation?
fields.explanation: Normal lymph nodes are firm, mobile, and non-tender. Hard, fixed nodes may indicate malignancy.

fields.text: Which of the following conditions is caused by elevated intraocular pressure?
fields.explanation: Glaucoma is characterized by elevated intraocular pressure that can damage the optic nerve and lead to vision loss.

fields.text: Which of the following is an absolute contraindication for the use of fibrinolytic therapy?
fields.explanation: Suspected aortic dissection is an absolute contraindication for fibrinolytic therapy as it can cause life-threatening bleeding.

fields.text: What is the usual treatment of Raynaud's Phenomenon?
fields.explanation: Calcium channel blockers are the usual treatment for Raynaud's phenomenon as they help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation.

fields.text: What is the most common pathogenic mechanism of acute pancreatitis?
fields.explanation: Autodigestion occurs when pancreatic enzymes are activated within the pancreas itself, leading to inflammation and tissue damage.

fields.text: What does the nurse monitor for in a patient with Acute Renal Failure?
fields.explanation: Acute renal failure can cause fluid overload leading to pulmonary edema and electrolyte imbalances causing ECG changes.

fields.text: A disease caused by Treponema pallidum is?
fields.explanation: Treponema pallidum is the spirochete bacterium that causes syphilis, a sexually transmitted infection.

fields.text: Which of the following is an important nursing intervention for a patient with Cushing Syndrome?
fields.explanation: Cushing syndrome causes immunosuppression due to excess cortisol, making patients susceptible to infections.

fields.text: Involvement of which structure will lead to right-sided hemiplegia and aphasia?
fields.explanation: Left middle cerebral artery supplies the left hemisphere speech centers and motor areas controlling the right side of the body.

fields.text: What is cyanosis?
fields.explanation: Cyanosis is a bluish discoloration of skin and mucous membranes due to inadequate oxygenation of blood.

fields.text: The period from conception to birth is denoted as?
fields.explanation: Prenatal period refers to the time from conception through pregnancy until birth.

fields.text: Who proposed the nursing theory of self-care deficit?
fields.explanation: Dorothea Orem developed the Self-Care Deficit Nursing Theory, focusing on patients' ability to perform self-care activities.

fields.text: The head circumference of a normal infant is?
fields.explanation: The normal head circumference of a full-term newborn is approximately 33-35 cm (13-14 inches).

fields.text: The leading cause of death in adolescence is?
fields.explanation: Accidents (unintentional injuries) are the leading cause of death among adolescents, including motor vehicle accidents.

fields.text: The body temperature at which tissues and cells can best function is?
fields.explanation: Normal body temperature ranges from 36-38°C (96.8-100.4°F), with optimal cellular function at around 37°C.

fields.text: The mechanism by which body loses heat to the environment without having direct contact is?
fields.explanation: Radiation is the transfer of heat through electromagnetic waves without direct contact between surfaces.

fields.text: What is the normal pulse rate of an infant?
fields.explanation: Normal infant pulse rate is 120-160 beats per minute, higher than adults due to smaller heart size and higher metabolic rate.

fields.text: What is the normal partial pressure of carbon dioxide in arterial blood?
fields.explanation: Normal arterial carbon dioxide partial pressure (PaCO2) is 35-45 mm Hg, indicating proper ventilation.

fields.text: What is the term used to denote regular, abnormally slow respirations?
fields.explanation: Bradypnea refers to abnormally slow breathing rate, typically less than 12 breaths per minute in adults.

fields.text: Inflammation of skin at the base of the nail is?
fields.explanation: Paronychia is inflammation of the soft tissue around the nail, often caused by bacterial or fungal infection.

fields.text: The condition in which both eyes do not focus on an object simultaneously is known as?
fields.explanation: Strabismus is a condition where the eyes do not align properly, preventing binocular vision.

fields.text: How many drops in a standard drip set makes one ml?
fields.explanation: A standard IV drip set delivers approximately 15 drops per milliliter, used for calculating IV flow rates.

fields.text: What is the intrinsic rate of the normal A-V node?
fields.explanation: The AV node has an intrinsic firing rate of 40-60 beats per minute when acting as the heart's pacemaker.

fields.text: How much sodium is normally present in the extracellular fluid?
fields.explanation: Normal serum sodium concentration is 135-145 mEq/L, essential for maintaining fluid balance and nerve function.

fields.text: The stool discharged from an ostomy is called?
fields.explanation: Effluent is the specific term for waste material that flows out of an ostomy (colostomy, ileostomy, or urostomy).

fields.text: Which of the following is a common cause of orthostatic hypotension in a bedridden patient?
fields.explanation: Prolonged bed rest leads to decreased blood volume and muscle deconditioning, causing orthostatic hypotension.

fields.text: Who was the first president of the International Council of Nurses?
fields.explanation: Mrs. Bedford Fenwick was the first president of the International Council of Nurses, established in 1899.

fields.text: Who introduced antiseptic spray during surgery?
fields.explanation: Joseph Lister introduced the use of carbolic acid (phenol) antiseptic spray during surgery in the 1860s.

fields.text: A large infection made up of several boils is known as?
fields.explanation: A carbuncle is a cluster of boils (furuncles) that form a connected area of infection under the skin.

fields.text: Which of the following is a vesicant drug?
fields.explanation: Dactinomycin is a vesicant chemotherapy drug that can cause severe tissue damage if it extravasates from the vein.

fields.text: Pain caused by damage to somatic tissue is known as?
fields.explanation: Nociceptive pain results from activation of nociceptors by tissue damage or inflammation in somatic structures.

fields.text: The separation and disruption of previously joined wound edges is termed as?
fields.explanation: Dehiscence is the partial or complete separation of wound edges that were previously joined by sutures or healing.

fields.text: What type of dressing is Duoderm?
fields.explanation: Duoderm is a hydrocolloid dressing that forms a gel when in contact with wound exudate, promoting moist wound healing.

fields.text: Contractures frequently occur after burn healing because of?
fields.explanation: Contractures develop due to excessive scar tissue formation during healing, which restricts range of motion.

fields.text: Association between HLA antigens and diseases is most commonly found in what disease conditions?
fields.explanation: HLA (Human Leukocyte Antigen) associations are strongest with autoimmune diseases due to immune system dysfunction.

fields.text: What is the most common cause of secondary immunodeficiencies?
fields.explanation: Drugs, particularly immunosuppressive medications and chemotherapy, are the most common cause of secondary immunodeficiencies.

fields.text: If a person is having an acute rejection of a transplanted organ, which drug would most likely be used?
fields.explanation: Daclizumab is a monoclonal antibody used for acute rejection episodes in organ transplant patients.

fields.text: The primary difference between benign and malignant neoplasm is?
fields.explanation: Malignant tumors are characterized by their ability to invade surrounding tissues and metastasize, unlike benign tumors.

fields.text: If blood plasma has higher osmolality than fluid within red blood cells, the mechanism involved in equalizing fluid concentration is?
fields.explanation: Osmosis is the movement of water across a semipermeable membrane from lower to higher solute concentration.

fields.text: Which symptom should be watched for in a patient receiving a loop diuretic?
fields.explanation: Loop diuretics can cause hypokalemia, leading to cardiac arrhythmias manifested as weak, irregular pulse.

fields.text: Which should be watched for in a patient who has just undergone total thyroidectomy?
fields.explanation: Positive Chvostek sign indicates hypocalcemia, which can occur after thyroidectomy due to inadvertent parathyroid gland damage.

fields.text: Which surgical procedure involves removal of a body organ?
fields.explanation: Mastectomy is the surgical removal of the breast, while the other options are incisions or reconstructive procedures.

fields.text: The cause of arcus senilis is?
fields.explanation: Arcus senilis is a gray or white arc around the cornea caused by cholesterol deposits, common in elderly.

fields.text: What is the cause of presbyopia?
fields.explanation: Presbyopia occurs due to age-related loss of lens flexibility, making it difficult to focus on near objects.

fields.text: Complete absence of melanin pigment in patchy areas on a patient's hand is described as?
fields.explanation: Vitiligo is an autoimmune condition causing complete loss of melanin in patches, resulting in white spots on skin.

fields.text: What instruction will you give to a patient after a chemical peel?
fields.explanation: Sun exposure should be avoided after chemical peels as the new skin is sensitive and prone to hyperpigmentation.

fields.text: Childhood atopic dermatitis is commonly seen in which body area?
fields.explanation: Atopic dermatitis in children commonly affects flexural areas like the antecubital space (inner elbow) and popliteal fossa.

fields.text: Which parameter is commonly used to assess adequacy of fluid replacement in a patient with burns?
fields.explanation: Urine output is the most reliable indicator of adequate fluid resuscitation in burn patients, with target of 0.5-1 ml/kg/hour.

fields.text: A diagnostic procedure which involves removal of pleural fluid for analysis is?
fields.explanation: Thoracentesis is the procedure to remove fluid from the pleural space for diagnostic or therapeutic purposes.

fields.text: Which helps to identify flail chest in a patient with chest trauma?

options.A: Multiple rib fractures seen in X-ray

fields.explanation: Flail chest is characterized by paradoxical movement where the affected segment moves inward during inspiration.

fields.text: Which is an intervention for a patient with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease?
fields.explanation: Pursed-lip breathing helps COPD patients by creating back pressure, keeping airways open longer during expiration.

fields.text: Which is the most common type of leukemia seen in older adults?
fields.explanation: Chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) is the most common type of leukemia in adults over 60 years of age.

fields.text: The blood vessel that brings blood to the right atrium is?
fields.explanation: The inferior vena cava (along with superior vena cava) returns deoxygenated blood to the right atrium.

fields.text: Which is the most common pathologic finding in individuals at risk for sudden cardiac death?
fields.explanation: Atherosclerotic heart disease (coronary artery disease) is the most common underlying pathology in sudden cardiac death.

fields.text: Which drug provides relief of anxiety and decreases both preload and afterload?
fields.explanation: Morphine sulfate reduces anxiety and causes vasodilation, decreasing both preload and afterload in heart failure.

fields.text: In a normal sinus rhythm, the measurement of an S-T segment is?
fields.explanation: The normal ST segment duration is approximately 0.12 seconds, representing the time between ventricular depolarization and repolarization.

fields.text: Which of the following is the most reliable method to assess pain in a conscious adult patient?
fields.explanation: Patient self-report is considered the gold standard for pain assessment in conscious, communicative patients as pain is a subjective experience.

fields.text: Which medication is contraindicated in patients with glaucoma?
fields.explanation: Atropine is contraindicated in glaucoma as it can increase intraocular pressure by causing pupil dilation and decreased aqueous humor drainage.

fields.text: The normal range for hemoglobin in adult males is?
fields.explanation: Normal hemoglobin levels in adult males range from 14-18 g/dL, which is higher than in females due to testosterone effects.

fields.text: Which of the following is the most common complication of immobility?
fields.explanation: Pressure ulcers are the most common complication of immobility, occurring due to prolonged pressure on skin and underlying tissues.

fields.text: Which position is most appropriate for a patient with difficulty breathing?
fields.explanation: Fowler's position (semi-upright) facilitates breathing by allowing maximum lung expansion and reducing work of breathing.

fields.text: The therapeutic range for digoxin is?
fields.explanation: The therapeutic range for digoxin is 0.8-2.0 ng/mL. Levels above 2.0 ng/mL increase the risk of toxicity.

fields.text: Which vitamin deficiency causes night blindness?
fields.explanation: Vitamin A deficiency causes night blindness (nyctalopia) as it is essential for the formation of rhodopsin in rod cells.

fields.text: The most common site for intramuscular injection in adults is?
fields.explanation: The ventrogluteal site is considered the safest and most commonly recommended site for IM injections in adults due to absence of major vessels and nerves.

fields.text: Which of the following is a sign of digitalis toxicity?
fields.explanation: Visual disturbances, including yellow-green halos around objects, are classic signs of digitalis toxicity.

fields.text: The medical term for difficulty in swallowing is?
fields.explanation: Dysphagia is the medical term for difficulty swallowing, which can occur due to various neurological or structural disorders.

fields.text: Which electrolyte imbalance is most commonly associated with muscle weakness and cardiac arrhythmias?
fields.explanation: Hypokalemia (low potassium) commonly causes muscle weakness and dangerous cardiac arrhythmias due to altered membrane potential.

fields.text: What is the normal range for adult respiratory rate?
fields.explanation: Normal adult respiratory rate is 12-20 breaths per minute. Rates outside this range may indicate respiratory distress.

fields.text: Which hormone is responsible for the regulation of blood glucose levels?
fields.explanation: Insulin is the primary hormone responsible for regulating blood glucose levels by facilitating glucose uptake by cells.

fields.text: The most important nursing intervention for a patient with neutropenia is?
fields.explanation: Neutropenia increases infection risk due to decreased white blood cells, making infection prevention the priority nursing intervention.

fields.text: Which of the following is the best indicator of fluid volume deficit?
fields.explanation: Urine output is the most reliable indicator of fluid volume status, with decreased output indicating fluid deficit.

fields.text: What is the most common cause of acute pancreatitis?
fields.explanation: Gallstones are the most common cause of acute pancreatitis, followed by alcohol abuse, accounting for about 80% of cases.

fields.text: Which medication is used as an antidote for acetaminophen overdose?
fields.explanation: N-acetylcysteine (NAC) is the specific antidote for acetaminophen overdose, helping to prevent hepatotoxicity.

fields.text: The normal range for platelet count is?
fields.explanation: Normal platelet count ranges from 150,000-450,000/μL. Counts below 150,000 indicate thrombocytopenia.

fields.text: Which of the following is a late sign of increased intracranial pressure?
fields.explanation: Bradycardia is a late sign of increased ICP, part of Cushing's triad along with hypertension and irregular breathing.

fields.text: Which type of isolation is required for a patient with pulmonary tuberculosis?
fields.explanation: Pulmonary tuberculosis requires airborne isolation due to transmission through airborne droplet nuclei.

fields.text: The most common complication of diabetes mellitus is?
fields.explanation: Diabetic nephropathy is the most common long-term complication of diabetes, leading to chronic kidney disease.

fields.text: Which of the following is the priority nursing diagnosis for a patient with acute myocardial infarction?
fields.explanation: Decreased cardiac output is the priority nursing diagnosis in MI as it directly affects tissue perfusion and can be life-threatening.

fields.text: The normal range for blood urea nitrogen (BUN) is?
fields.explanation: Normal BUN levels range from 10-20 mg/dL. Elevated levels may indicate kidney dysfunction or dehydration.

fields.text: Which of the following is the most common cause of seizures in adults?
fields.explanation: Epilepsy is the most common cause of recurrent seizures in adults, though other causes should be considered in new-onset seizures.

fields.text: Which medication is used to treat severe allergic reactions?
fields.explanation: Epinephrine is the first-line treatment for severe allergic reactions (anaphylaxis) due to its rapid onset and life-saving effects.

fields.text: The most appropriate site for checking pulse in an infant is?
fields.explanation: The brachial artery is the most appropriate site for checking pulse in infants as it's easily accessible and reliable.

fields.text: Which of the following is a contraindication for lumbar puncture?
fields.explanation: Increased intracranial pressure is a contraindication for lumbar puncture due to risk of brain herniation.

fields.text: The primary purpose of the Glasgow Coma Scale is to assess?
fields.explanation: The Glasgow Coma Scale is used to assess level of consciousness and neurological function based on eye, verbal, and motor responses.

fields.text: Which of the following is the most common cause of hospital-acquired infections?
fields.explanation: Poor hand hygiene is the most common cause of hospital-acquired infections, making hand washing the most important prevention measure.

fields.text: The antidote for warfarin overdose is?
fields.explanation: Vitamin K is the antidote for warfarin overdose, helping to reverse anticoagulation by promoting clotting factor synthesis.

fields.text: Which of the following is the most reliable sign of cardiac arrest?
fields.explanation: Absence of pulse is the most reliable sign of cardiac arrest, indicating lack of effective cardiac output.

fields.text: The normal range for serum creatinine in adults is?
fields.explanation: Normal serum creatinine levels range from 0.6-1.2 mg/dL in adults. Elevated levels indicate kidney dysfunction.

fields.text: Which of the following is the most common cause of chronic kidney disease?
fields.explanation: Diabetes mellitus is the leading cause of chronic kidney disease, followed by hypertension.

fields.text: The medication used to reverse the effects of benzodiazepine overdose is?
fields.explanation: Flumazenil is the specific antidote for benzodiazepine overdose, acting as a competitive antagonist at GABA receptors.

fields.text: Which of the following is the most important nursing intervention for a patient with acute stroke?
fields.explanation: Maintaining airway patency is the priority in stroke patients as they may have compromised swallowing and cough reflexes.

fields.text: The normal range for white blood cell count is?
fields.explanation: Normal white blood cell count ranges from 4,000-11,000/μL. Values outside this range may indicate infection or blood disorders.

fields.text: Which of the following is the most common side effect of opioid analgesics?
fields.explanation: Constipation is the most common side effect of opioid analgesics and typically does not resolve with continued use.

fields.text: Which of the following is the most common nosocomial infection?
fields.explanation: Urinary tract infections are the most common nosocomial infections, often associated with catheter use.
